HTML:
At DFDS, we use AI to solve concrete operational problems across European logistics, ferry, and terminal operations.

We are looking for a Data Scientist focused on Applied AI and optimisation, someone who enjoys turning complex, real-world challenges into robust and scalable solutions that deliver measurable impact.

This is not a research role. This is about building solutions that are used.

About The Role

You will join the Data Science Chapter and AI Center of Excellence (CoE), where we both enable AI adoption across the organisation and build applied AI solutions for real operational problems.

In this role, your primary focus will be on developing and delivering AI solutions that support decision-making in logistics, ferry, and terminal operations.

You will work on turning complex operational challenges into deployable systems within machine learning, optimisation, and generative AI.

At the same time, you will contribute to a shared foundation of best practices, reusable patterns, and continuous learning across the team.

You will collaborate closely with product owners and engineers, while the chapter provides support for deployment, monitoring, and scaling.

What Success Looks Like

In your first 6–12 months, you will:

  • Contribute to a model or solution that is integrated into a product or workflow
  • Help shape how we apply AI and optimisation in practice at DFDS

What You Will Do

  • Translate operational problems into analytical and modelling approaches.
  • Develop ML models, statistical methods, and optimisation solutions
  • Design and build AI products leveraging agentic workflows where they create real value
  • Build MVPs and iterate them into production with engineers
  • Collaborate with stakeholders, such as ship’s crew or terminal workers and communicate results clearly.

About The Team

You will be part of DFDS’ Technology & Innovation division (T&I), working in a collaborative and experienced environment across data science, optimisation, and engineering.

The Data Science Chapter And AI CoE Provide

  • Shared standards for MLOps and responsible AI
  • Peer sparring and code/model reviews
  • A modern stack (Python, APIs, k8s based CI/CD pipelines, and MLOps tooling)

Example of problems that we are working on

  • Optimising route planning across logistics networks
  • Reducing fuel consumption through predictive and optimisation models
  • Predicting overbooking in freight and passenger systems
  • Forecasting invoice payment behaviour
  • Designing agent-based planning tools for terminals
  • Automating structured data extraction using LLMs and RAG

About You

We are looking for an early- to mid-career professional with strong analytical foundations.

Your background educational background can be many, e.g. a M.sc. or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, or similar.

You Most Likely Bring

  • ~2–4 years of industry experience working with ML/statistical models
  • Strong skills in modelling, mathematics, and problem formulation
  • Experience with designing, training and implementing of predictive models or optimisation algorithms in a production environment.

Your strengths

  • You enjoy turning real-world problems into structured models that delivers value
  • You appreciate the practices that keep models reliable in production (CI/CD, monitoring, deployment)
  • You are comfortable working across ML, AI, statistics, and optimisation
  • You are curious about LLMs and modern AI — but not driven by hype
  • You can prototype solutions in Python and collaborate with engineers on production

We think the strongest teams are built on diversity and inclusivity. Contrasting opinions, varied skills, and strong characters bring out the best in us all. And we cannot wait for you to join.

Working at DFDS

Join the movement and grow with us

We promote a great workplace with a diverse and vibrant community. Our award-winning headquarters in Copenhagen offers a modern workspace with ocean views and easy access to public transport.

Beyond the day-to-day work, DFDS has a strong social and community culture, including employee sports clubs, choir, and other shared activities — as well as on-site facilities that make it easy to connect with colleagues across teams.

You Will Join An Environment Focused On

  • Real-world impact
  • Learning and experimentation
  • Continuous development through T&I’s career development model, GROW

Are we a match? Then what are you waiting for? Apply now.

If you think your personality, skills, and experience match this role, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your CV and a brief motivational letter (in English) on why you are applying as soon as possible as we review applications on an ongoing basis.

For questions, please contact Data Science Chapter Lead, Per Hansen at pehan@dfds.com.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Please note that due to the summer holiday period, there might be a delay in response time, but rest assured that we will get back to you as soon as possible.

Title: Data Scientists for Big Data, IoT and AI projects
Description:
Stillingsbeskrivelse

Do you like working creatively with data analytics, big data, AI, visualisation, NoSQL and databases? Would you like to join an international organisation where we already work with data analytics and machine learning projects? Join BEUMER Group and exploit the chance to innovate, develop and test concepts for products and tools and most importantly, develop your own skill.

You will team up with colleagues, so you can share and grow your knowledge within the data science field. It also means focusing on using data from an already existing infrastructure where we collect data from more than 350 sites worldwide.

Join our Customer Diagnostics Center

You become part of our Data Analytics department, which is part of our Customer Diagnostics Center (CDC). In the CDC, which is part of our Customer Support division, we are tasked with making sure our installed base of systems around the globe are healthy and running smoothly.

Here, you join a well-established but agile team of highly sought for data scientists. You have the freedom to develop solutions across the organisation focusing on creativity and innovation on the conceptual stages of possible new products, services and tools.

In the CDC, you will find a multitude of skills and competences, and we enjoy an informal atmosphere where we combine our strengths to create brand new solutions for colleagues and customers. Moreover, you become part of a data analytics forum where we work closely with universities to expand our knowledge.

Create value through data across the organisation

Through a continuous dialogue with our business and product development, you will be responsible for defining new products, services and tools based on data and machine learning. We are a large organisation with several specialist fields, which means that we can enjoy the value of data analysis across disciplines.

Your Tasks Will Include


Initiating mining of existing data from material handling systems running in production, looking for possible bottlenecks, optimizations etc.
Processing, cleansing, and verifying the integrity of data used for analysis.
Participating in the maintenance of the existing data pipeline.
Participate in defining and evaluating new techniques for data collection and analysis.
Drive the rollout and extension of our data collection to all sites in the BEUMER Group.


We have a strong preference for open-source software and are working with platforms and tools such as Kubernetes, ArgoCD, MLFlow, vLLM, Elastic stack, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, Numaflow, Python/Java/Golang, Jupyter notebooks and Opentelemetry. You will test and implement prototypes, e.g. for customer solutions or for your colleagues to provide them with the right tools to perfect their work. As such, you bridge the worlds of product development, subject specialists and technology to innovate the business.

Experience with data driven decision making, big data and concept development?

Role

Most important to this role is that you have a good all-round technical foundation, allowing you to implement software, configure tools, understand our domain and be a sounding board in the creative process. To do so, we also imagine that you:


Hold a master's degree or PhD as a computer scientist, engineer, physicist or similar.
Have a great understanding of and hands-on experience with using data analytics for system and concept development.
Speak and write English fluently and it is a big plus if you understand Danish as well.


Personality-wise, you are a strong communicator who can work across the organisation and with customers. Your strength is within innovation and creative thinking, which shows itself in added value in the concept development process and tests.

Interested?

If you have any questions regarding this position, please do not hesitate to contact Head of Global Data Analytics Anders Lildballe at +45 30 17 93 48. Please apply online via beumergroup.com/career.

We look forward to meeting you.

BEUMER Group

Conveying, loading, palletising, packing, sortation and distribution – BEUMER Group is a leader within the development and manufacture of high-technological intralogistics systems for global markets. Our employees stand out from others thanks to their ability to supply innovative solutions to our customers. They can do this because they know the industry and are passionate about continuously developing and expanding their knowledge. We support these high standards through teamwork, mutual respect and a working culture based on trust that fosters stability and security for all our employees. Our common goal is to implement outstanding and innovative projects worldwide.

Title: Unsolicited: Business Intelligence & Data Science
Description:
Work With BI/data Science In An International Software Company
Are you motivated by making a difference and contributing to tomorrow’s BI/data science solutions? In which case you might be our new colleague.

You will create a strong foundation for strategic decisions
In your Business Intelligence or data science role, you will be working with various solutions that support Systematic’s business areas.

This involves the development of products and solutions for our customers and other business units – all in close collaboration with highly skilled colleagues.

Some of the tasks you will be working with involve:


Identifying and understanding the needs of both the customer and the product organisation through close dialogue
Using data to create value for our customers
Developing, coding and implementing BI/data science solutions
Cleaning and analysing data, and implementing and developing machine learning models
Facilitating workshops and building data models that support domains, user behaviour and software
Being part of a dynamic working environment characterised by collaboration, development and quality.



You can expect to work with a range of technologies, such as Microsoft Power BI, Python, Azure Cloud, Microsoft SQL Server, T-SQL, Microsoft SSIS and SSAS, data warehousing, data modelling and analysis.

High level of interdisciplinary expertise and collaboration
We are committed to acquiring knowledge and developing methods based on a well-functioning group of BI/data science colleagues.

We enjoy a very high level of job satisfaction, and the dynamics between experienced experts and young talents contribute to a high level of development and progress.

We work closely together across the different disciplines and areas of expertise, with teams comprising project managers, consultants, data scientists, software architects and business developers.

A key characteristic of the teams at Systematic is that we dare to challenge and develop ourselves and each other. We focus on knowledge-sharing in our work, and always find more innovative ways of doing things.

You understand the importance of BI and data science
You are creative, good at spotting opportunities and think out of the box. You have a solid understanding of the interplay between people, technologies and processes.

You have strong communication skills combined with sound business acumen, and you enjoy being involved in a wide variety of assignments.

You stay calm when faced with numerous tasks and requirements, and you maintain an overview and understand how to prioritise the tasks at every stage of the project.

You are organised, analytical and you are driven by the quality of your work. You enjoy presenting new ideas and have what it takes to garner the support of your colleagues and business partners.

At the same time, you are a team player who enjoys the prospect of actively contributing to the team and participating in development activities.

You are fluent in English, both orally and in writing. It is an advantage if you speak Danish, but this is not a requirement.

We work with security classified projects and therefore, as a Systematic employee, you need to have security clearance. Read more here:
https:
//systematic.com/en-gb/careers/security-clearance/ .

Any questions?

Please feel free to get in touch by emailing us at job@systematic.com. Unsolicited applications will continually be assessed according to current vacancies.
We store your details for up to six months, and if in that period your profile matches our requirements for a new job, you will be contacted to arrange an interview.



Connect with us!

Title: Data Scientist, AI Fashion Planning
Description:
Join BESTSELLER TECH and revolutionise fashion with machine learning!

Do you want to be part of shaping the future of fashion? BESTSELLER TECH is looking for an experienced and skilled individual to join our AI Fashion Planning team as a Data Scientist.

Become a part of our mission to drive data-driven fashion innovation. Your key to success in this role is your passion for solving business problems using data, algorithms and advanced AI technologies in close collaboration with your team and domain experts.

YOUR TEAM

By joining our AI Fashion Planning team, you will be at the forefront of developing state-of-the-art AI solutions for some of BESTSELLER’s most strategically important projects within demand planning and inventory optimisation. Our work has a high focus on delivering value to our brands and we are working in a hypothesis-driven manner to always test out ideas and directly measure the impact of our solutions.

AI is a key part of how we work in AI Fashion Planning, and it’s accelerating. We are looking for data scientists with real habits and judgement on AI agents, modern developer tools and their effective application in a data science setting. We do this because we want to learn faster, verify better and ship high-quality data science products, not because we want more code quicker.

YOUR JOB


Develop and implement advanced machine learning models for demand forecasting and inventory optimisation.
Analyse large datasets to extract meaningful insights and inform business strategies.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business needs and deliver effective AI solutions.
Continuously research and stay updated on the latest AI and machine learning technologies relevant to fashion recommendations.
Present complex data and results in a clear and understandable manner to stakeholders.


Your Benefits


You will be working in one of the world’s largest fast fashion companies, and our AI Fashion Planning team is at the forefront of innovation.
We believe in empowering our team members to explore new ideas, solve complex challenges, and make a real impact.
You will be part of a vibrant, supportive environment with passionate colleagues and plenty of opportunities to grow your career.
You will be building data science products using modern AI tools.


YOUR PROFILE

We are looking for an experienced data scientist with significant developing and shipping data science products for real use cases and solving real business cases. More specifically, we’re looking for the following qualifications:


A Master’s degree or Ph.D. in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics or a related field.
3-5 years of post-academic experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer.
Proven experience in machine learning, predictive modelling, and data analysis.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Experience in Databricks.
Strong communication skills, both verbal and written.


APPLYING WITH AN AI AGENT

If you are using a coding agent (Codex, Claude, Cursor, Pi, OpenCode, or similar), we would love to see how you make it work. Point your agent at our show-your-work skill and include the artifact it produces in your cover letter. It is optional, but it is the clearest way to show us how you actually work with AI.

READY TO MAKE A DIFFERENCE?

If you are excited by the opportunity to shape the future of fashion through machine learning solutions, we would love to hear from you! Apply today or no later than 6 August 2026, and join us on our journey to transform the industry.

If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to reach out to Jakob Blaavand on +45 21 68 49 41.

We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to take down the ad when we find the right candidate.

LOCATION: Aarhus, Denmark.

About Bestseller Tech

We are more than 400 people strong, located in Denmark, Spain and Rwanda. We are excited about what we do, but there’s more to us than our tech stack. We are young at heart and thrive in a flat organization. Our teams are agile and empowered with end-to-end responsibility. We want to be at the forefront of the newest technologies, and we move forward together. Every day. If you want to play a significant part in empowering our business, then welcome to BESTSELLER TECH. BESTSELLER TECH is part of the worldwide fashion company BESTSELLER. For more information, please go to www.bestseller.com.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Place: Billund Municipality, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Just imagine turning manufacturing and engineering data into actionable insights that elevate every LEGO® brick’s quality. Then make it real!

This Senior Data Scientist role sits within Data & Automation under Advanced Engineering at our Billund site. You’ll be building predictive data systems, drive quality assurance, enable process improvements and much more to stabilize production workflows across element and mould manufacturing.

Play your part in our team succeeding

We have unified many of the strong digital capabilities existing in Elements & Additive Engineering (E&AE), by combining Computer Aided Engineering (CAE), Data Science, and Software Automation capabilities into one unit - Advanced Engineering. It is our goal to elevate these capabilities and reinforce their importance and impact in the operational value chain.

Our ambition is to deploy these capabilities in E&AE and beyond, while creating a strong center of excellence in Data & Automation (D&A) within Advanced Engineering to secure critical digital capabilities for the future.

D&A is a young team that creates data insights for data-driven decision making on all organizational levels. We improve product quality and strengthen processes through data analysis. Also, we reduce tedious process steps to enable value-adding engineering productivity initiatives through low code / no code automation platforms for Engineering & Algorithmic modelling software.

In short - it is where digital simulations meet data science and process automation.

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be essential for the D&A team to reach our goals, enable us to make the right prioritizations, leading the digital change and being a strong role model – not only for the team but also for our stakeholders and collaborators.


Core Responsibilities





Predictive Modeling Drive the development of models to predict process quality, potential defects, and forecast maintenance schedules using techniques like statistical analysis, machine learning and AI models.
Quality Assurance Drive the implementation of quality control algorithms and anomaly detection methods to monitor the operations processes and identify deviations or defects.
Process Optimization Collaborate with engineers and value chain teams to optimize operations processes based on data-driven insights, aiming to understand and improve process stability and element and mould quality, as well as enabling faster technology development cycles.
Communication and Visualization Ensure effective communication of findings and insights through data visualization and reports to cross-functional teams, stakeholders and external partners to develop operations solutions
Data Cleaning and Preprocessing Responsible for the preparation of collected data by cleaning, organizing, and transforming it into a suitable format for visualization and analysis. Assess large data quantities and ensure the dimensional reduction of these data is handled in a way that minimizes the consequences of this reduction.
Root Cause Analysis Engage in investigating and identifying the root causes of process issues or failures by analyzing historical data, helping in continuous improvement efforts.
Capabilities Building Support in building capabilities within the LEGO Group to develop and enhance data science skills, knowledge and expertise to expand our Advanced Engineering roadmap and meet organizational goals.
Data Collection Support in setting up data extractions methods to gather data from various existing sources. Also, identify data weaknesses and recommend where to secure sensor data, machine logs, and quality control measurements.




Do you have what it takes?





Background in statistical modelling and data analysis spanning two or more years
Strong analytical skills with the ability to analyze, understand and illustrate data
Experience in programming such as Python, R, etc. as well as experience with data visualization platforms such as Power BI,
Experience in working with cloud services, such as Microsoft Azure, and data engineering platforms such as Databricks
Strong ability to navigate complex data from various sources as well as experience with ETL (Extract, Transform, Load)
A team player that strives to succeed in an ever-changing and challenging environment
Organized and structured, independent, self-driven and proactive attitude
Excellent communication and organizational skills
Experience with GitHub
Ability to construct Machine Learning models
Advanced English




Nice to Have





Experience with Big Data, Manufacturing Data
Knowledge of Additive Manufacturing and Injection Moulding technologies
Flare for innovation, development, and out of the box thinking




No relocation assistance is offered for this position

#

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. HTML:
Job Description

Just imagine turning manufacturing and engineering data into actionable insights that elevate every LEGO® brick’s quality. Then make it real!

This Senior Data Scientist role sits within Data & Automation under Advanced Engineering at our Billund site. You’ll be building predictive data systems, drive quality assurance, enable process improvements and much more to stabilize production workflows across element and mould manufacturing.

Play your part in our team succeeding

We have unified many of the strong digital capabilities existing in Elements & Additive Engineering (E&AE), by combining Computer Aided Engineering (CAE), Data Science, and Software Automation capabilities into one unit - Advanced Engineering. It is our goal to elevate these capabilities and reinforce their importance and impact in the operational value chain.

Our ambition is to deploy these capabilities in E&AE and beyond, while creating a strong center of excellence in Data & Automation (D&A) within Advanced Engineering to secure critical digital capabilities for the future.

D&A is a young team that creates data insights for data-driven decision making on all organizational levels. We improve product quality and strengthen processes through data analysis. Also, we reduce tedious process steps to enable value-adding engineering productivity initiatives through low code / no code automation platforms for Engineering & Algorithmic modelling software.

In short - it is where digital simulations meet data science and process automation.

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be essential for the D&A team to reach our goals, enable us to make the right prioritizations, leading the digital change and being a strong role model – not only for the team but also for our stakeholders and collaborators.


Core Responsibilities



  • Predictive Modeling Drive the development of models to predict process quality, potential defects, and forecast maintenance schedules using techniques like statistical analysis, machine learning and AI models.
  • Quality Assurance Drive the implementation of quality control algorithms and anomaly detection methods to monitor the operations processes and identify deviations or defects.
  • Process Optimization Collaborate with engineers and value chain teams to optimize operations processes based on data-driven insights, aiming to understand and improve process stability and element and mould quality, as well as enabling faster technology development cycles.
  • Communication and Visualization Ensure effective communication of findings and insights through data visualization and reports to cross-functional teams, stakeholders and external partners to develop operations solutions
  • Data Cleaning and Preprocessing Responsible for the preparation of collected data by cleaning, organizing, and transforming it into a suitable format for visualization and analysis. Assess large data quantities and ensure the dimensional reduction of these data is handled in a way that minimizes the consequences of this reduction.
  • Root Cause Analysis Engage in investigating and identifying the root causes of process issues or failures by analyzing historical data, helping in continuous improvement efforts.
  • Capabilities Building Support in building capabilities within the LEGO Group to develop and enhance data science skills, knowledge and expertise to expand our Advanced Engineering roadmap and meet organizational goals.
  • Data Collection Support in setting up data extractions methods to gather data from various existing sources. Also, identify data weaknesses and recommend where to secure sensor data, machine logs, and quality control measurements.



Do you have what it takes?



  • Background in statistical modelling and data analysis spanning two or more years
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to analyze, understand and illustrate data
  • Experience in programming such as Python, R, etc. as well as experience with data visualization platforms such as Power BI,
  • Experience in working with cloud services, such as Microsoft Azure, and data engineering platforms such as Databricks
  • Strong ability to navigate complex data from various sources as well as experience with ETL (Extract, Transform, Load)
  • A team player that strives to succeed in an ever-changing and challenging environment
  • Organized and structured, independent, self-driven and proactive attitude
  • Excellent communication and organizational skills
  • Experience with GitHub
  • Ability to construct Machine Learning models
  • Advanced English



Nice to Have



  • Experience with Big Data, Manufacturing Data
  • Knowledge of Additive Manufacturing and Injection Moulding technologies
  • Flare for innovation, development, and out of the box thinking



No relocation assistance is offered for this position

Title: Data Scientist – Commodities – Leading Quantitative Hedge Fund – Aarhus – Excellent Compensation + Bonus
Description:
I am working with a leading global quantitative investment firm that is looking to hire a Data Scientist to join its Commodities business in Aarhus.




This is a front-office facing role sitting at the intersection of Research, Trading, and Engineering, focused on transforming complex datasets into high-value research and trading inputs used across systematic commodities strategies.




The successful candidate will work closely with traders, quantitative researchers, and data engineers to onboard, transform, and productionise both traditional and alternative datasets that directly support investment decisions.




What you'll be working on

Partnering with Trading and Research teams to understand and deliver data requirements
Designing and implementing scalable data pipelines supporting systematic commodities strategies
Onboarding and integrating new datasets into the firm's research platform
Developing data transformation processes to create high-value, investment-ready datasets
Building tools and workflows to extract, clean, normalise, and aggregate data from diverse raw sources and formats
Managing the end-to-end lifecycle of new data acquisitions and integrations
Driving data quality, validation, and monitoring across critical research datasets
Troubleshooting complex data challenges and reducing time-to-production for new data sources
Researching and implementing innovative methods for data extraction and acquisition
Working closely with stakeholders across Trading, Research, Engineering, and external data vendors




This is a highly visible position offering direct exposure to front-office teams and the opportunity to work on some of the most interesting data challenges within quantitative investing.




What they're looking for

3+ years of experience in Data Science, Data Engineering, Quantitative Analytics, or a related data-focused role
Advanced Python programming skills, including strong experience with Pandas and NumPy
Experience designing and maintaining data pipelines and data transformation workflows
Experience working with financial, market, commodities, or alternative datasets
Postgraduate degree in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, or another quantitative discipline
Demonstrable interest in financial markets and data-driven investment processes
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-performance environment
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and business teams
Prior experience within systematic trading, quantitative finance, hedge funds, asset management, commodity trading, market data, or alternative data environments would be advantageous




Compensation

Highly competitive base salary
Discretionary performance bonus
Comprehensive benefits package
Relocation support available where appropriate




The search is active, and the firm is looking to move quickly for high-calibre candidates.




To apply, respond to this advert or contact me directly for a confidential discussion at gala.santaballa@mondrian-alpha.com.




All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence.

Title: Join Halfspace’s AI & Advanced Analytics Team - Shaping the Future with AI
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Who We Are

At Halfspace, Part of Accenture - Center for Advanced AI - we believe that the smallest edge can make the biggest difference. We began in sports, predicting outcomes in live football matches, and today we bring that same precision and ambition into the world of business. We're here to unlock value through artificial intelligence and help organisations make better, more informed decisions, faster.

With more than one hundred AI and data projects delivered, our work has transformed industries and earned international recognition. But awards and numbers tell only part of the story. What truly defines us is our people. Our team comes from leading institutions such as KU, DTU, Stanford, Oxford, Harvard, Caltech, Yale, Colombia and many more, blending expertise in physics, engineering, mathematics, statistics, operations research, computer science, chemistry, economics, biology, design and strategy. Together, we share curiosity, boldness, and a pioneering spirit.

Halfspace is a specialized spearheading unit within Accenture, focused on bringing the deepest expertise in applied AI. We are 'Inch Wide - Mile Deep' in our capabilities.

What You'll Be Doing

As part of the AI & Advanced Analytics team at Halfspace, you’ll be working at the cutting edge of artificial intelligence — designing, building, and deploying systems that push what’s possible. You’ll contribute to the development of intelligent agents, generative AI models, and advanced analytical frameworks that deliver real-world impact across industries.

You’ll work alongside engineers, scientists, and strategists from some of the world’s leading institutions, applying deep technical expertise and creative thinking to solve complex problems. From prototyping novel AI applications to operationalizing models at scale, you’ll bridge research and engineering to turn ideas into solutions that redefine performance.

Every experiment, model, and insight you create will help organizations make better decisions, faster — embodying our mission to deliver the smallest edge that makes the biggest difference.

Who We're Looking For

We’re looking for curious and technically exceptional individuals who live at the intersection of AI, mathematics, and software. You combine a strong foundation in statistical modeling and machine learning with the creativity and rigor to turn theory into working systems.

You’re fluent in Python and comfortable working with modern AI stacks — whether that means fine-tuning large language models, architecting agentic systems, or building robust analytical pipelines.

For our Generative AI unit we value:



Strong proficiency in Python and core AI libraries

Experience working with integrating Generative AI and developing AI Agents or other applications

Intellectual curiosity, precision, and a pioneering mindset — the qualities that define Halfspace



For our Advanced Analytics unit we value:



Strong proficiency in Python and core ML libraries

Solid understanding of statistical modeling, optimization, and experimental design

Familiarity with MLOps and model deployment practices

Intellectual curiosity, precision, and a pioneering mindset — the qualities that define Halfspace



If you have less than two years of professional experience, please include your academic transcripts with your application.

Life at Halfspace, part of Accenture

We think and act like a professional sports team. We value flexibility, trust, and balance. We invest in learning and development to keep our team ahead of the curve on everything AI. And we create an environment where ambitious people can grow while working on projects that matter, side by side with some of the world’s leading companies and greatest minds.

About Halfspace, Part Of Accenture

Halfspace was established in 2015. We're on a mission to bring the power of artificial intelligence to industry and deliver exceptional quality and value, whenever we engage, while creating a space which attracts the brightest, most curious and most talented people. As of March 2025 Halfspace became a part of Accenture to help build and grow the Accenture Center for Advanced AI across the Nordics and EMEA.

Title: Quantitative Researcher
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Quantitative Researcher




Are you a quantitatively minded problem-solver who enjoys turning complex financial data into robust, systematic strategies? Do you combine strong mathematical thinking with practical programming skills — and have you already applied them in a financial markets context?




A leading quantitative investment team is looking for a Quantitative Researcher to help design, develop, and improve equity statistical arbitrage strategies. This role sits at the intersection of research and development — requiring both strong quantitative reasoning and the ability to build reliable research tools and models in code.




This is an opportunity to work in a collaborative, intellectually rigorous environment where ideas are tested quickly and engineering quality matters.




What You'll Do:

📊 Develop Quantitative Strategies – Research and implement systematic equity statistical arbitrage models using large financial datasets.

🧪 Experiment and Iterate – Design research experiments, evaluate signals, and refine strategies through rigorous analysis and testing.

💻 Build Research Tools – Write clean, efficient Python code to process data, develop models, and support the research pipeline.

📈 Work with Financial Data – Analyse complex datasets to identify patterns, inefficiencies, and opportunities in financial markets — particularly fixed income and equity instruments.

🤝 Collaborate with Engineers and Researchers – Work closely with a small team of technically strong colleagues to translate ideas into production-ready strategies.




The Team:

You'll join a lean quantitative research group focused on systematic trading strategies. The environment is highly technical but pragmatic — ideas are judged by evidence, and researchers are expected to both design models and build the tools needed to test them. The team values people who combine theoretical insight with the ability to implement their ideas in code.




What They're Looking For:

Strong quantitative background (e.g. Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or similar)
Experience applying quantitative or ML methods to financial data — whether in systematic trading, derivatives, risk modelling, or a related area of financial markets
Strong Python skills and experience working with large datasets
Evidence of practical software development experience
Ability to move between theory and implementation
This is not a role where financial markets knowledge can be learned on the job — some prior exposure to financial instruments or trading environments is expected




You may come from industry or academia, but should be able to point to concrete experience working with financial data or within a financial markets context.




What They Offer:

Highly competitive compensation with strong bonus potential
Pension, healthcare, and comprehensive benefits
A collaborative environment with direct impact on live trading strategies
The opportunity to work on challenging quantitative problems with high-quality data and infrastructure




💰 Base salary is 60-90 Danish Krona per month (€120K – €150K equivalent per year) and benefits include bonus

Title: Algorithm Developer in Mathematical modelling
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Do you want your engineering skills to directly shape how athletes understand and improve their movement? Are you excited by the opportunity of turning noisy, high-dimensional data into clean, actionable biomechanical metrics used by professionals and ambitious amateurs alike?

We are looking for an Algorithm Developer in Mathematical modelling to join our Motion Analysis team. You’ll work at the intersection of deep learning and real-world sports science, taking the raw output of our AI models and building the modelling and post-processing pipelines that make the data real. This is where the engineering meets the athlete.

As a member of our team, you will be responsible for the key step between AI prediction and product output. Our deep learning models produce body key points from video. Your job is to turn those noisy, uncertain estimates into robust skeletal fits and reliable biomechanical metrics that our users can trust and act on.

We believe it takes careful post-processing and modelling to turn raw deep learning predictions into data that is real, reliable, and actionable. You will design and develop custom algorithms that handle outlier detection, optimization, and human body model fitting, often as one integrated modelling approach rather than separate steps. You will be part of an agile, collaborative team, contributing to solutions from prototype to production and helping shape our products. You will join a team of six, with diverse backgrounds spanning deep learning, computer vision, software engineering, and mathematical modelling.

We work in a collaborative environment where team members support each other and share responsibility for moving projects forward. We believe that strong solutions emerge through open dialogue across disciplines. We also value a positive and engaging work environment, where curiosity is encouraged and people can explore problems that interest them alongside supportive colleagues.

Your Primary Responsibilities Will Include


Designing and implementing mathematical models that fit skeletal structures to noisy key point data, balancing input uncertainty with anatomical constraints.
Developing robust outlier detection and rejection methods that work within the modelling framework, not just as a pre-processing step.
Building and refining optimization pipelines for post-processing deep learning outputs into accurate biomechanical metrics.
Conducting data analysis and statistical validation to understand model performance and propose improvements.
Collaborating closely with our deep learning engineers and software engineers to ensure your models integrate cleanly into the broader product pipeline.
Exploring and evaluating new approaches from research literature, adapting them to our specific problem domain and data characteristics.


Job Requirements


A master's degree or PhD in Applied Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Signal Processing, or a related technical field with strong mathematical foundations.
Solid skills in mathematical modelling, you are comfortable formulating real-world problems as optimization, estimation, or fitting problems.
Strong foundations in linear algebra, statistics, and numerical methods.
Experience working with noisy, real-world data,you understand uncertainty and know how to handle it.
Programming skills in Python - Familiarity with C++ is a plus.
You have an entrepreneurial mindset and are driven by making your work matter in a real product, not just in a report.
Communication skills, with the ability to explain mathematical choices to colleagues from different technical backgrounds.


Preferably


Experience with signal processing methods such as filtering, smoothing, or time-series analysis.
Familiarity with constrained optimization techniques and solvers.
Experience with human body modelling, biomechanics, or motion capture data.
Exposure to deep learning, not necessarily training models, but understanding what they output and why they fail.
A genuine passion for applying mathematical thinking to solve real-world challenges.
An interest in sports like golf, baseball, or others is a bonus!


The Office

We are located at DTU Science Park in Hørsholm. We take great pride in the state-of-art building we have designed! The office features bright meeting rooms, coffee areas to catch up with colleagues, a large testing arena with simulators available for employees and a fitness center, everything surrounded by a beautiful forest and respecting the environment. We made sure to utilize the newest technologies to be energy efficient and be mindful of ventilation and sound propagation. The cherry on top is our canteen: with an indoor and outdoor area to enjoy the fresh food prepared by our own kitchen staff.

Join the home of a powerful sports brand and a one-of-a-kind technology

Our proprietary technology is based on expert knowledge about radar, computer vision, data, and software engineering. Our solutions are developed by specialists who endlessly explore and challenge new technical boundaries. Trackman’s blend of cutting-edge technology, sports, and continued growth make our company an outstanding place to work.

Our work culture is entrepreneurial, ambitious, and rewarding as you get to collaborate with inspiring colleagues and interact with the leaders of golf, baseball, and football. Based on a unique brand reputation, we are proud to work in close partnership with the top athletes, organizations, and teams that rely on our technology to stay ahead of the race.

We are a Danish company founded, owned, and managed since 2003 by three entrepreneurs with a strong passion for sports. Today, we have a global footprint with our headquarters located in Denmark (Hørsholm) and offices in New York, Phoenix, Seoul and Tokyo. We employ over 1000+ people worldwide, half of which work in Denmark, where most hardware and software products are developed, tested, and manufactured.

Where innovation happens

At Trackman, we know that great people make great products. We believe that teams thrive, and innovation sparks in an atmosphere where people are encouraged to think for themselves, where everybody’s voices are heard, and where the best ideas prevail in the pursuit of exceptional solutions. This mix makes Trackman an inspiring company – for our customers and colleagues.

Share our Passion!

Applications are continuously assessed, so please send your application as soon as possible.

  • An interest in sports like golf, baseball, or others is a bonus!

The Office

We are located at DTU Science Park in Hørsholm. We take great pride in the state-of-art building we have designed! The office features bright meeting rooms, coffee areas to catch up with colleagues, a large testing arena with simulators available for employees and a fitness center, everything surrounded by a beautiful forest and respecting the environment. We made sure to utilize the newest technologies to be energy efficient and be mindful of ventilation and sound propagation. The cherry on top is our canteen: with an indoor and outdoor area to enjoy the fresh food prepared by our own kitchen staff.

Join the home of a powerful sports brand and a one-of-a-kind technology

Our proprietary technology is based on expert knowledge about radar, computer vision, data, and software engineering. Our solutions are developed by specialists who endlessly explore and challenge new technical boundaries. Trackman’s blend of cutting-edge technology, sports, and continued growth make our company an outstanding place to work.

Our work culture is entrepreneurial, ambitious, and rewarding as you get to collaborate with inspiring colleagues and interact with the leaders of golf, baseball, and football. Based on a unique brand reputation, we are proud to work in close partnership with the top athletes, organizations, and teams that rely on our technology to stay ahead of the race.

We are a Danish company founded, owned, and managed since 2003 by three entrepreneurs with a strong passion for sports. Today, we have a global footprint with our headquarters located in Denmark (Hørsholm) and offices in New York, Phoenix, Seoul and Tokyo. We employ over 1000+ people worldwide, half of which work in Denmark, where most hardware and software products are developed, tested, and manufactured.

Where innovation happens

At Trackman, we know that great people make great products. We believe that teams thrive, and innovation sparks in an atmosphere where people are encouraged to think for themselves, where everybody’s voices are heard, and where the best ideas prevail in the pursuit of exceptional solutions. This mix makes Trackman an inspiring company – for our customers and colleagues.

Share our Passion!

Applications are continuously assessed, so please send your application as soon as possible.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Place: Herlev, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About The Job

You will join a growing AI team of 11 talented colleagues with a shared mission: transforming data and AI into measurable business value for Flatpay and our merchants.

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will design, build, and productionize GenAI-powered products and pipelines that support business-critical decisions across the organization. A key area of focus will be customer-facing conversational AI, where creativity, strong product thinking, and a user-centred mindset are essential for developing cutting-edge solutions.

In addition, you will work on AI-driven document, image, and audio processing systems that power critical business processes and analytics. You will collaborate closely with the broader AI team while taking strong ownership of complex projects and driving them from ideation to scalable production systems.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex, ambiguous problems with modern AI technologies and who has the technical judgment to turn ideas into robust, maintainable, and high-impact products. You will work across the entire AI product lifecycle — from data engineering and model development to deployment and user-facing applications — while helping set a high standard for applied machine learning, LLMs, and generative AI at Flatpay.

We are looking for someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment, takes end-to-end ownership, and is comfortable engaging directly with stakeholders to identify opportunities, shape solutions, align technical direction with business needs, and continuously drive improvement. As a senior member of the team, you will also be expected to contribute to technical direction, raise the quality bar, and support the growth of those around you.

What You'll Do


Design, build, and maintain production-grade GenAI applications and pipelines using Python, MLflow, Databricks, and leading AI platforms such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.
Develop AI-powered solutions including customer-facing conversational AI, LLM-driven data enrichment and analytics workflows, and audio, image, and multimodal processing systems for both internal and customer-facing products.
Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested production Python code, including automated tests, monitoring, guardrails, validation frameworks, and fail-safe mechanisms to ensure reliability, security, and robustness in AI systems.
Build, orchestrate, and maintain data and AI workflows in Databricks, including pipelines, jobs, workflows, model serving endpoints, and custom applications deployed on the Databricks platform.
Leverage cloud platforms such as GCP, AWS, and Azure to provision, deploy, and scale AI services, including LLMs.
Integrate, optimize, and manage AI workflows that leverage external platforms and services, including tools such as ElevenLabs, Metabase, and other third-party providers.
Collaborate closely with data engineers to ensure AI products are built on reliable, scalable, and well-governed data foundations.
Drive complex projects from proof of concept through production deployment, monitoring, ongoing optimization, and business adoption.
Make technical design decisions, evaluate trade-offs, and help define best practices for building reliable, secure, and scalable AI systems.
Mentor and support other team members through code reviews, technical sparring, and sharing practical knowledge across AI engineering, machine learning, and production GenAI systems.


Who You Are


You have 5+ years of industry experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, AI Engineering, or a similar technical role, with a strong track record of delivering production AI or ML systems.
You have hands-on experience building and maintaining customer-facing GenAI applications and pipelines in production and understand the importance of automated testing, monitoring, guardrails, and reliability in production AI systems.
You have experience using LLMs from multiple state-of-the-art providers of frontier and open-source models, and you have a deep understanding of the strengths and trade-offs of different frontier and open-source models.
You have strong experience building applications that leverage retrieval-augmented generation, tool calling, agents, or other modern LLM architectures.
You are highly comfortable developing production solutions in Python and working with libraries such as Transformers and the SDKs of leading LLM providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and others.
You are comfortable working in Databricks or similar cloud-based data and AI platforms, including building pipelines, workflows, model serving endpoints, and production deployments.
You have strong PySpark, Pandas, and SQL skills and are comfortable working with large, structured datasets.
You are pragmatic and able to turn ambiguous business problems into concrete technical solutions, while helping stakeholders understand trade-offs and opportunities.
You are comfortable taking ownership of high-impact projects, working independently, and collaborating directly with stakeholders across the business.
You contribute positively to team standards, technical direction, and knowledge sharing, and you enjoy helping others become better at building high-quality AI systems.


Why Flatpay?


Impact: Ship AI and data solutions that create real impact in one of Europe’s fastest-growing companies.
Meaningful products: Build models that help merchants and colleagues make better decisions, act on insights, and improve workflows.
Learning and growth: Develop across forecasting, production ML, data engineering, and AI in a supportive team.
Flat structure: Flat structure and a strong action-over-process culture
Competitive salary
Vacation: 6 weeks paid
Relaxed atmosphere with optional social events such as minigolf evenings, hackathons, and company parties in exciting off-site locations.
Co-workers with a passion for data and AI, and a passion for sharing knowledge daily.
Pension and health insurance, as well as almost unlimited company merch.


About Us

Welcome to Flatpay!

Flatpay is not just another payment solution; we are dedicated to innovating the market for payment solutions and financial services in the SME segment with our customer-centric and transparent services.

Today, we are one of the fastest-growing fintech companies out of the Nordics - and Denmark's fastest unicorn!

Flatpay is more than just work. We believe in fostering a vibrant and fun work culture where our ambitious colleagues can thrive. As part of our team, you will be one of +2.000 passionate colleagues dedicated to making a difference in the world of payments.

What sets Flatpay apart is our dedication. We continuously strive to stay ahead of the curve, adapting our solutions to meet our client's evolving needs.

Let's shape the future of merchants together!

Title: Quantitative Developer - Python
Description:
Qube Research & Technologies (QRT) is a global quantitative and systematic investment manager, operating in all liquid asset classes across the world. We are a technology and data driven group implementing a scientific approach to investing. Combining data, research, technology, and trading expertise has shaped our collaborative mindset, which enables us to solve the most complex challenges. QRT’s culture of innovation continuously drives our ambition to deliver high quality returns for our investors.




You will join a quantitative development function that works closely with Researchers, Traders and Cloud Engineering teams to build applications, data pipelines and analytics that support research and trading workflows. The role combines software engineering, quantitative development and data engineering, with responsibility for developing production applications, supporting research infrastructure and integrating large scale datasets.




Your future role within QRT

Design, develop, deploy and maintain cloud native Python applications supporting research and trading workflows, including market data dashboards, risk analytics, profit and loss reporting, weather analytics and performance analysis
Develop quantitative tools including backtesting frameworks, optimisation tools, pricing libraries and ETL pipelines for integrating and validating alternative datasets
Build and maintain scalable data pipelines that support model development and production workflows
Monitor, support, debug and enhance existing research and trading infrastructure
Work closely with Researchers, Traders and Engineering teams to deliver production quality solutions that meet evolving business requirements




Your present skillset

At least three years of professional Python development experience
Strong knowledge of Python, including numerical libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, Polars and Xarray
Strong quantitative mindset with an interest in working closely with trading and research functions
Experience developing user interfaces or an interest in building data visualisation applications such as Plotly, Dash or Streamlit is beneficial
Experience with AWS or another cloud platform together with infrastructure as code technologies (e.g. Lambda, S3, DynamoDB, RDS or AWS CDK is advantageous)
Good knowledge of relational databases such as PostgreSQL together with object storage technologies
Experience developing ETL pipelines and working with large scale datasets
Strong understanding of software engineering practices including version control, automated testing, packaging and deployment
Ability to work independently while collaborating closely with business and technology stakeholders to deliver production solutions
Experience with parallel or distributed computing is advantageous




QRT is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome diversity as essential to our success. QRT empowers employees to work openly and respectfully to achieve collective success. In addition to professional achievement, we are offering initiatives and programs to enable employees achieve a healthy work-life balance.

Title: Senior Data Scientist - Store Partnering
Place: Albertslund, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Senior Data Scientist – Store Partnering

Coop Danmark, Albertslund (Possibility of remote work)

Do you want to be a key driver in establishing an ambitious new team where decisions are founded in thorough data analysis and where machine learning forecasting methodologies are at the heart of the roadmap? Then this is your opportunity to make your mark on the future of the supply chain at Coop!

Your new team

In the role of Senior Data Scientist you will become a central part of the new Store Partnering team. The team will be heavily focused on spearheading a new approach to data centricity in Coop, and as the team is newly formed, you will be highly able to influence ways of working.

About The Position

As a Senior Data Scientist in Coop’s new Store Partnering team, you will have a central responsibility for establishing and developing the data-driven approach to supply chain processes and decisions. Your focus will be on driving the digital agenda forward in Coop’s supply chain, with the aim of improving the quality and efficiency of planning processes. Focus will be on leveraging the vast data landscape available to a large retail organization - turning data into valuable forecasting and planning action. You will work to make technology, data science and AI a pivotal component in how we do planning to unlock efficiencies in planning processes and improvements to consumer availability for millions of consumers every day.

Your Primary Responsibilities


Tools: Developing promotion of quantity estimation methodologies and related tools. Develop the analytics toolbox that makes campaign and store planning tasks faster to execute.
Data driven culture and problem solving: Drive the agenda of putting analysis, math and statistics behind every decision by supporting the store and demand planning team with advanced analysis for operational decisions and development initiatives.
Measurement & Learning: Help establish the KPI and reporting framework, such that continuous development enables and learnings from campaigns and operations can be reused.
RELEX AI: Driving the ML/AI agenda in Relex and operationalizing relevant large data and AI agent use cases. Includes design, diagnostics, and roadmap development.
Guidance for analysts and specialists: Coaching and sparring with the cohort of analysts and specialists across supply chain planning.


About You

You have solid experience with complex analysis, and you thrive on translating data into concrete improvements. You are analytically strong, structured, and have a natural ability to collaborate across disciplines. You enjoy continuously developing ways of working and are energized about the opportunity to be part of shaping up a new team with a big organizational focus. You are driven by creating structure and momentum in complex deliverables and thrive at the intersection of business, IT, and operations. You can communicate complex statistical and technical concepts and findings for the broader organization.

We imagine that you have several of the following competencies:


4–6 years of experience with applied ML in production environment, preferably within a supply chain or retail context.
Master or PHD within mathematical fields
Strong acumen in data science programming language like Python, R or similar data science programming language and experience with MLOps – model versioning, backtesting, and retraining pipelines
Experienced in feature engineering on demand data, including causal factors such as pricing, promotions, events, and weather
Able to translate complex models into business value and communicate technical results to non-technical stakeholders
Has experience with Relex or similar advanced planning systems


Coop Offers

An exciting role in a highly ambitious environment where you will have the opportunity to make a real difference. You will become part of a dedicated team with a lot of hunger and high professional expertise and strong collaboration, where you help shape the digital agenda in Coop’s supply chain.


Influence on projects that impact our 900 stores and 38,000 colleagues
An attractive compensation package including pension, health insurance, and employee benefits
Discounts in Coop's stores, summer cottage rentals, staff clubs, and purchasing schemes
Flexible working hours and the option of one work-from-home day per week


Your base will be at Coop's head office in Albertslund, close to the teams you collaborate with on a daily basis.

Interested?

Are you ready to help shape the future of data-driven decision-making at one of Scandinavia's largest retailers? Then send your CV via our online application form no later than 03-08-2026. We conduct interviews on an ongoing basis, so don't hesitate to apply.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact Supply Chain Manager Bo Bøgvad by email at bo.boegvad@coop.dk

You can read more about Coop as a workplace at www.coop.dk.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Senior Model Developer
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us in this role where you’ll develop and improve core market models that support short- and mid-term analysis. You’ll combine strong Python development with an interest in energy markets to deliver scalable, reliable models used daily by trading and commercial teams.

Welcome to Forecasting & Market Analysis

You’ll be part of Forecasting & Market Analysis where you together with your colleagues will develop models and forecasts supporting trading and portfolio decisions across power and gas. You’ll work closely with analysts, traders, and platform teams to ensure high-quality data-driven insights. As a team, we collaborate across disciplines, share knowledge, and continuously improve how we model and understand energy markets.

You’ll play an important role in:


Developing our new gas supply and demand to enable our gas trading and portfolio management activities
developing and improving gas and power market models for supply-demand and price formation.
maintaining and operating power production forecast models and related tooling.
integrating key drivers such as weather, fuel prices, outages, and interconnector flows into models.
ensuring model outputs reflect market behaviour across short- and mid-term horizons.
improving model governance, monitoring performance, and keeping assumptions and data up to date.
collaborating with traders, analysts, and developers to deliver actionable insights and recommendations.


To succeed in the role, you:


have a background in quantitative development, engineering, economics, or similar, or equivalent experience.
write scalable, well-tested Python code and apply modern development practices.
are familiar with version control, CI/CD, and collaborative software development.
work confidently with data, including time series analysis, SQL, and data visualisation tools.
are motivated to build expertise in energy markets and translate it into models.
communicate clearly and work effectively both independently and as part of a team.


Maybe you’ve read the above and can see you have some transferable skills, even though they don’t quite match all the points. If you think you can bring something to the team, we still encourage you to apply.

Shape the future with us

Send your application to us as soon as possible. We’ll be conducting interviews on a continuous basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we’ve found the right candidate.

As an applicant or employee, you may request reasonable work and position accommodation or adjustments via accommodation@orsted.com.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.

Position salary range:

For this position, the expected base pay range is kr51,440.00- kr77,160.00 for the advertised location. The offered base salary will be determined within this range based on relevant skills, experience, and expected role fulfilment within the stated pay range to ensure a fair and competitive offer. Where applicable, you may also be eligible for variable payment and a comprehensive benefits package.

Our benefits package:

For this position, employees who meet eligibility requirements have access to: employer‑paid pension (11% contribution), full insurance coverage (accident, life, critical illness, and health insurance); five weeks of paid holiday plus 10 additional paid days off per holiday year; a 3% holiday allowance; access to Ørsted Life & Fitness Vitality including fitness classes, physiotherapy, massage, and wellbeing programmes; a multimedia package (mobile phone, PC, internet); staff club membership with sport and cultural activities; and access to the company canteen (monthly lunch scheme or daily tickets).

Pre-employment background check:

Certain positions may require a background check as part of the hiring process. If applicable, you will be informed during the recruitment process. Background checks are conducted by an external provider in accordance with local legal requirements.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.

Position salary range:

For this position, the expected base pay range is kr51,440.00- kr77,160.00 for the advertised location. The offered base salary will be determined within this range based on relevant skills, experience, and expected role fulfilment within the stated pay range to ensure a fair and competitive offer. Where applicable, you may also be eligible for variable payment and a comprehensive benefits package.

Our benefits package:

For this position, employees who meet eligibility requirements have access to: employer‑paid pension (11% contribution), full insurance coverage (accident, life, critical illness, and health insurance); five weeks of paid holiday plus 10 additional paid days off per holiday year; a 3% holiday allowance; access to Ørsted Life & Fitness Vitality including fitness classes, physiotherapy, massage, and wellbeing programmes; a multimedia package (mobile phone, PC, internet); staff club membership with sport and cultural activities; and access to the company canteen (monthly lunch scheme or daily tickets).

Pre-employment background check:

Certain positions may require a background check as part of the hiring process. If applicable, you will be informed during the recruitment process. Background checks are conducted by an external provider in accordance with local legal requirements.

Title: AI Engineer Consultant
Description:
Introduction

Vil du hjælpe nogle af Danmarks største virksomheder med at realisere deres AI-potentiale? Vi søger en nysgerrig og ambitiøs AI Engineer til vores konsulentteam, som brænder for at omsætte data og AI til konkrete forretningsresultater. Rollen spænder bredt - fra klassisk data science og machine learning til de nyeste muligheder inden for Generativ AI og AI-agenter.

IBM Client Innovation Center er en del af IBM Consulting og leverer rådgivning, udvikling og implementering af innovative teknologiløsninger til både danske og internationale virksomheder. Vi kombinerer dyb teknisk ekspertise med stærk forretningsforståelse og hjælper vores kunder med at accelerere digital transformation, innovation og værdiskabelse.

Hos os får du mulighed for at arbejde med de nyeste AI-teknologier og være med til at udvikle løsninger, der skaber reel værdi for nogle af Danmarks mest spændende organisationer.

Your Role And Responsibilities

Som AI Engineer hos os bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor vi hjælper kunderne med at løfte deres tekniske fundament og skabe målbar værdi gennem AI. Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med kunderne om at designe, udvikle og implementere løsninger, der spænder fra dataplatforme og Databricks-pipelines til machine learning-modeller, AI-løsninger og LLM-baserede AI-agenter.

Hos os får du det bedste fra begge verdener. Mange af vores projekter løber over flere år og giver mulighed for at skabe langsigtet værdi for kunden, mens andre er kortere og mere intensive. Det betyder, at du løbende bliver eksponeret for nye brancher, teknologier og forretningsudfordringer - og udvikler dig hurtigt både fagligt og personligt.

Dine Opgaver Vil Blandt Andet Være At


Arbejde med de nyeste teknologier inden for AI, Generativ AI og Agentic AI.
Designe, udvikle og operationalisere machine learning- og AI-løsninger i produktionsmiljøer.
Udvikle intelligente applikationer ved hjælp af LLM’er, RAG, AI-agenter og Copilot-teknologier.
Bygge robuste data- og AI-pipelines på moderne cloud-platforme som Azure og Databricks.
Samarbejde tæt med kunder om at omsætte forretningsmæssige udfordringer til skalerbare tekniske løsninger.
Bidrage til arkitektur, teknologivalg og best practices inden for moderne AI-udvikling.
Holde dig opdateret på nye teknologier og aktivt bidrage til at styrke vores AI-kompetencer og innovationsarbejde.


Required Technical And Professional Expertise

For at få succes i rollen forestiller vi os at du:


Har erfaring inden for data engineering, data science eller AI – typisk 3-4 års erfaring eller er en stærk profil med potentiale til hurtigt at udvikle sig mod seniorniveau.
Har solid forståelse for machine learning, deep learning, AI (herunder Generativ og Agentic AI) samt stærke kompetencer i Python og SQL.
Har kendskab til Databricks og moderne data- og AI-arkitekturer.
Har praktisk erfaring med Azure Data & AI-platformen og trives med at bygge robuste løsninger i pro-code miljøer fremfor low-code/no-code.
Er nysgerrig, læringsorienteret og motiveres af tæt samarbejde med både kunder og kolleger.
Kommunikerer klart og professionelt på både dansk og engelsk.


Vi tilbyder

Hos IBM får du mulighed for at arbejde i frontlinjen af teknologi og innovation. Du bliver en del af et fællesskab, hvor læring, videndeling og personlig udvikling er en naturlig del af hverdagen. Samtidig får du adgang til et stærkt netværk og mulighed for at lære af nogle af branchens mest erfarne specialister og ledere.

Desuden Tilbyder Vi


Et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor ekspertise går hånd i hånd med et inkluderende og socialt fællesskab.
Adgang til et af branchens stærkeste læringsmiljøer med træning, certificeringer og kontinuerlig kompetenceudvikling gennem IBMs globale læringsplatform for mere end 250.000 IBM-medarbejdere, så du hele tiden kan udvikle dig i takt med nye teknologier, markeder og kundebehov.
Spændende projekter, der giver variation, ansvar og mulighed for at skabe reel værdi fra dag ét.
Dygtige kolleger og en ledelse, der prioriterer både faglig udvikling, trivsel og arbejdsglæde.
Fleksibilitet og frihed under ansvar i et dynamisk og tillidsbaseret arbejdsmiljø.
En arbejdsplads med base på Aarhus Ø samt mulighed for at arbejde tæt sammen med kunder på tværs af brancher og lokationer.


Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, så er du velkommen til at kontakte Data & AI Practice Lead, Anders Schaumann på anders.schauman@ibm.com.
