Title: Data Analytics Specialist
Description:
Job Description:

What the job is:

At Satair , data and analytics are among our most powerful levers for driving a culture of continuous improvement across the entire organization. They play a vital role in enhancing operational efficiency, boosting competitiveness, engaging employees, and most importantly, delivering outstanding customer satisfaction.

As a Data Analytics Specialist , you will join our dynamic Data & Infrastructure team , where we are dedicated to empowering the business with self-service tools, robust data foundations, and scalable cloud infrastructure solutions. Our mission also includes upskilling teams across the organization to build long-term capabilities and promote a data-driven mindset.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with functional teams across all areas of the Satair organization. You’ll act as a trusted advisor, partnering with business analysts and domain experts to provide technological insights, define data standards, conduct diagnostics, and apply advanced analytics to optimize processes and maximize value for our customers, as well as making sure the underlying infrastructure and pipelines are running smoothly and as effective as possible.

Our team is truly global, reflecting Satair’s international footprint with colleagues and offices all across the globe. This means you will work in small, agile, cross-functional teams, focused on delivering practical, high-impact solutions side-by-side with the business.

Task and responsibilities:


Improve and maintain our key pipelines running on the Airbus Skywise platform - a customized version of Palantir Foundry. Main tasks will be focusing on optimisation, automation and improving the backend pipelines and flows.
Support the business with in-depth data analysis and experimentation to uncover valuable business insights and support data-driven decision-making.
Engage with functional teams to understand end-to-end processes, identify pain points, and uncover opportunities for improvement through data and emerging technologies.
Translate analysis into actionable outcomes by developing automated tools, visual dashboards, reports, or workflows that enhance productivity and business value.
Design and deliver Minimum Viable Products (MVPs) to quickly test, validate, and address critical business challenges.
Act as a coach and advisor to cross-functional teams, guiding them on data quality improvement plans, and supporting them in developing their own MVPs and analytics use cases.


How will you contribute to the team:

We are looking for a dynamic, business-savvy individual who is passionate about data and technology, with a strong drive to deliver impactful results through collaboration and innovation.


You are a quick learner, passionate about your work and driven to get things done with a collaborative way of working.
You bring a pragmatic, solution-oriented mindset-comfortable with rapid experimentation and iteration when building digital products and tools.
You understand the intersection of data and business processes and are passionate about solving real-world industrial and operational challenges.
Robust coding abilities (Python, SQL, Java) & experience in working with APIs, algorithms, data structures would be a plus.
Experience with DevOps tools / modern cloud stack & practices would be a plus.
Experience working with Data Mesh principles such as decentralized data ownership, domain-aligned data products, and self-serve data infrastructure would be a strong plus.
Prior experience with Patantir Foundry would be a big plu
A university degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field is preferred.
3-5 years of experience in an analytics, data science, or similar role within a complex business environment.
Strong knowledge of English, both orally and in writing


What does your next challenge look like?

At SATAIR, we believe in sustainable aviation that allows people to travel and connect across the globe.

We are on a mission to provide world class material solutions that keep aircraft flying and extend their lifespan.

In Satair, you can challenge the status quo, pioneer new ways of working, and generate real value for airlines and suppliers worldwide.

Being part of the Airbus family gives us the opportunity to live a global mindset, working in international and diverse teams and collaborating across functions, sites and cultures.

If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow, making an impact on aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!

About SATAIR

Satair is an Airbus Services company offering first-class material management solutions in the aviation aftermarket industry. We take pride in delivering an excellent customer experience around the clock, driving innovation, and contributing to aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

With our global functional organisation spanning 12 sites and locations worldwide, and a team of over 2,000 people supporting a $3.9 billion turnover, you will be part of a truly global company that continues to grow year after year.

Welcome to the Satair family!

If you want to know more about our business, have a closer look at our website www.SATAIR.com or check out our SATAIR Youtube Channel at www.youtube.com/sataircompany !

What do we offer at SATAIR?

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!

Here is a selection of some of the benefits we offer our employees in Copenhagen:

Financial Benefits: Competitive salary, Employee Stock Ownership Program, Success sharing, Health insurance, Group accident insurance, Employee discounts to local businesses

Employee Health and Wellbeing: On-site gym, App-based health & wellbeing platform, Amazing canteen at a subsided price, Massage scheme

Learning and Development: Airbus Leadership University, Large number of e-learning and training offers for personal and professional development, Promotion of professional development (e.g. postgraduate studies, technician training), Possibility of mentoring, Gain international experience

Work-life blend and life phase support: Possibility to work remotely, 4 care days, Senior days, Working from home policy (2 days remote per week)

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company

Please send us your application as soon as possible to get the opportunity to become part of an exceptional global team!

We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.

When applying for the position, you accept our data privacy statement: http://www.SATAIR.com/privacy

This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.

Company:

Satair A/S

Contract Type:

Permanent

Experience Level:

Professional

Job Family:

Digital

By submitting your CV or application you are consenting to Airbus using and storing information about you for monitoring purposes relating to your application or future employment. This information will only be used by Airbus.

Airbus is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.

Airbus is, and always has been, committed to equal opportunities for all. As such, we will never ask for any type of monetary exchange in the frame of a recruitment process. Any impersonation of Airbus to do so should be reported to emsom@airbus.com .

At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking.

Title: Lead AI Developer
Description:
Hos Envidan udvikler vi softwareløsninger, der gør en forskel for vandforsyning, spildevand og klimatilpasning. Nu tager vi næste skridt og integrerer AI direkte i vores produkter – og derfor søger vi en dygtig person, der kan lede den rejse.

Om rollen

Du bliver vores primære drivkraft på AI og dataanalyse. Du arbejder tæt sammen med vores softwareudviklere, produktteam, data arkitekt og rådgivere om at identificere, designe og implementere AI-funktionalitet, der skaber reel forskel for vores kunder i forsyningssektoren.

Du er ikke typen, der venter på at få opgaver stukket i hånden. Du opsøger aktivt nye muligheder – i dialog med kunder, kollegaer og potentielle eksterne partnere indenfor AI – og du forstår vigtigheden af at sætte AI i produktion på en ansvarlig og bæredygtig måde.

Det er en rolle med stor frihed og bred indflydelse – du er med fra idé til deployment og drift.

Dine ansvarsområder


Proaktivt opsøge og kvalificere relevante AI use cases i tæt dialog med kunder og resten af forretningen
Integrere LLM-baserede løsninger i vores eksisterende softwareplatform, fx intelligente søgefunktioner, automatiske opsummeringer og smarte assistenter til brugerne
Opbygge og forvalte en solid AI governance-ramme for løsninger, der sættes i produktion – herunder ansvarlig AI, datakvalitet og monitorering
Samarbejde tæt med vores data arkitekt om datamodeller, infrastruktur og arkitektur der understøtter AI-løsningerne
Udvikle og vedligeholde AI-pipelines og dataflows i et produktionsmiljø
Samarbejde med produktteamet om brugervenlig AI-integration der føles naturlig, ikke påklistret
Vurdere og implementere eksterne analysemoduler fra partnere, og sikre at de integreres på en ansvarlig og bæredygtig måde i vores platform
Bidrage til vores dataanalysekapacitet – herunder datavisualisering og simple forecast modeller, eksplorative analyser og sparringspartner.


Hvem er du?

Vi forestiller os, at du har en uddannelse indenfor datalogi, software engineering, matematik/statistik eller data science, men har du den rette erfaring og et stærkt teknisk fundament, er din formelle baggrund dog ikke afgørende.

Du har 3–5 års erfaring inden for data science, ML‑engineering eller AI‑produktudvikling og har:


Solid erfaring med LLMs og generativ AI – herunder prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning eller API- integration (fx OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral)
Praktisk erfaring med at integrere AI i eksisterende softwaresystemer og produktionsmiljøer og herunder gerne erfaring med softwareudvikling
Kendskab til eller erfaring med AI governance, ansvarlig AI og best practices for AI i produktion
Stærke Python-færdigheder og kendskab til relevante frameworks (LangChain, FastAPI, Pandas, m.fl.)
Erfaring med cloud (gerne Azure) og gerne erfaring med Infrastructure as Code; du er tryg ved at deployere og drifte løsninger i et cloud-miljø
Interesse for eller erfaring fra forsynings-, industri- eller infrastruktursektoren er et plus


Som person trives du i et miljø hvor du tænker både strategisk og skriver kode. Du er proaktiv og opsøgende af natur – du finder selv mulighederne og bringer dem i spil. Som ansvarlig for AI i Envidan Software, evner du at kommunikere tekniske løsninger klart til både kunder og ikke-tekniske kollegaer.

Hvad tilbyder vi?


En nøglerolle i et team på 28 engagerede kollegaer, hvor vi er i gang med at re-designe vores eksisterende applikationer – og nu også integrere AI som centralt element
En arbejdskultur præget af samarbejde, tillid og faglig sparring – vi hjælper hinanden og deler viden, både i dagligdagen og gennem fælles learning sessions
Tæt samarbejde med erfarne softwareudviklere og direkte adgang til produktbeslutninger
Mulighed for at sætte dit præg på produkter, der bruges i kritisk infrastruktur


Vi har interesse i hinanden og vægter et godt socialt miljø højt – udlevet gennem teamarrangementer, faglige klubber og en aktiv personaleforening på tværs af hele organisationen



Stillingen er fuldtid. Vi tror på, at de bedste løsninger opstår når mennesker mødes – og vi lægger vægt på at skabe en stærk sammenhængskraft i teamet. Derfor ser vi gerne, at du er på kontoret i Silkeborg 2–3 dage om ugen. De resterende dage er der fuld fleksibilitet til at arbejde hjemmefra.

Har du spørgsmål til rollen er du velkommen til at kontakte Afdelingschef Thorben Føns på +45 4117 7627 eller thf@envidan.dk. Henvendelser fra rekrutteringsbureauer frabedes.

Ansøgning

Vil du med ombord, så send din ansøgning snarest muligt. Vi behandler ansøgninger fortroligt og løbende, og vi lukker stillingsopslaget, når vi har fundet den rette kandidat.

Om os

Envidan er Nordens ledende vand- og spildevandsspecialist med tre selvstændige forretningsområder: rådgivning, entreprise og software.

Vi hjælper forsyninger, kommuner og industri inden for vandets kredsløb. Og uanset om vi rådgiver om klimatilpasning, leverer færdige anlæg eller udvikler innovative softwareløsninger, er vandfaglighed og digital tænkning altid omdrejningspunktet.

Fra vores kontorer i Norge, Sverige og Danmark bidrager +500 medarbejdere til at løse nogle af vandsektorens største udfordringer: Bæredygtig vandhåndtering, klimatilpasning og sikring af rent drikkevand.

På den måde er vores forretning født med et bæredygtigt DNA. Men vores ambitioner rækker videre. Via vores stærke, tekniske faglighed kan vi anvise praktiske, bæredygtige løsninger på vores kunders konkrete problemer.

Envidan – en del af Rambøll

Hos Envidan bliver du en del af Nordens stærkeste hold af specialister inden for vandets kredsløb. I partnerskab med forsyninger, kommuner og industrier yder vi rådgivning, leverer anlæg og skaber software. Disse tre forretningsområder samlet gør os stærkere, fordi vores kunder får bedre software af en leverandør, der forstår sektoren. Og bedre rådgivning af en leverandør, der også forstår at bygge anlæg.

Du får sektorens skarpeste hoveder som kolleger. Det kan vi tilbyde, fordi vi hvert år allokerer 20 procent af vores overskud i forskning og udvikling.
Show more
HTML:
Hos Envidan udvikler vi softwareløsninger, der gør en forskel for vandforsyning, spildevand og klimatilpasning. Nu tager vi næste skridt og integrerer AI direkte i vores produkter – og derfor søger vi en dygtig person, der kan lede den rejse.

Om rollen

Du bliver vores primære drivkraft på AI og dataanalyse. Du arbejder tæt sammen med vores softwareudviklere, produktteam, data arkitekt og rådgivere om at identificere, designe og implementere AI-funktionalitet, der skaber reel forskel for vores kunder i forsyningssektoren.

Du er ikke typen, der venter på at få opgaver stukket i hånden. Du opsøger aktivt nye muligheder – i dialog med kunder, kollegaer og potentielle eksterne partnere indenfor AI – og du forstår vigtigheden af at sætte AI i produktion på en ansvarlig og bæredygtig måde.

Det er en rolle med stor frihed og bred indflydelse – du er med fra idé til deployment og drift.

Dine ansvarsområder

  • Proaktivt opsøge og kvalificere relevante AI use cases i tæt dialog med kunder og resten af forretningen
  • Integrere LLM-baserede løsninger i vores eksisterende softwareplatform, fx intelligente søgefunktioner, automatiske opsummeringer og smarte assistenter til brugerne
  • Opbygge og forvalte en solid AI governance-ramme for løsninger, der sættes i produktion – herunder ansvarlig AI, datakvalitet og monitorering
  • Samarbejde tæt med vores data arkitekt om datamodeller, infrastruktur og arkitektur der understøtter AI-løsningerne
  • Udvikle og vedligeholde AI-pipelines og dataflows i et produktionsmiljø
  • Samarbejde med produktteamet om brugervenlig AI-integration der føles naturlig, ikke påklistret
  • Vurdere og implementere eksterne analysemoduler fra partnere, og sikre at de integreres på en ansvarlig og bæredygtig måde i vores platform
  • Bidrage til vores dataanalysekapacitet – herunder datavisualisering og simple forecast modeller, eksplorative analyser og sparringspartner.

Hvem er du?

Vi forestiller os, at du har en uddannelse indenfor datalogi, software engineering, matematik/statistik eller data science, men har du den rette erfaring og et stærkt teknisk fundament, er din formelle baggrund dog ikke afgørende.

Du har 3–5 års erfaring inden for data science, ML‑engineering eller AI‑produktudvikling og har:

  • Solid erfaring med LLMs og generativ AI – herunder prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning eller API- integration (fx OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral)
  • Praktisk erfaring med at integrere AI i eksisterende softwaresystemer og produktionsmiljøer og herunder gerne erfaring med softwareudvikling
  • Kendskab til eller erfaring med AI governance, ansvarlig AI og best practices for AI i produktion
  • Stærke Python-færdigheder og kendskab til relevante frameworks (LangChain, FastAPI, Pandas, m.fl.)
  • Erfaring med cloud (gerne Azure) og gerne erfaring med Infrastructure as Code; du er tryg ved at deployere og drifte løsninger i et cloud-miljø
  • Interesse for eller erfaring fra forsynings-, industri- eller infrastruktursektoren er et plus

Som person trives du i et miljø hvor du tænker både strategisk og skriver kode. Du er proaktiv og opsøgende af natur – du finder selv mulighederne og bringer dem i spil. Som ansvarlig for AI i Envidan Software, evner du at kommunikere tekniske løsninger klart til både kunder og ikke-tekniske kollegaer.

Hvad tilbyder vi?

  • En nøglerolle i et team på 28 engagerede kollegaer, hvor vi er i gang med at re-designe vores eksisterende applikationer – og nu også integrere AI som centralt element
  • En arbejdskultur præget af samarbejde, tillid og faglig sparring – vi hjælper hinanden og deler viden, både i dagligdagen og gennem fælles learning sessions
  • Tæt samarbejde med erfarne softwareudviklere og direkte adgang til produktbeslutninger
  • Mulighed for at sætte dit præg på produkter, der bruges i kritisk infrastruktur

Vi har interesse i hinanden og vægter et godt socialt miljø højt – udlevet gennem teamarrangementer, faglige klubber og en aktiv personaleforening på tværs af hele organisationen



Stillingen er fuldtid. Vi tror på, at de bedste løsninger opstår når mennesker mødes – og vi lægger vægt på at skabe en stærk sammenhængskraft i teamet. Derfor ser vi gerne, at du er på kontoret i Silkeborg 2–3 dage om ugen. De resterende dage er der fuld fleksibilitet til at arbejde hjemmefra.

Title: Ansvarlig for data science og analyse (Data scientist)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Lige nu leder vi efter dig, der kan omsætte kvantitative data til meningsfulde indsigter, som understøtter Clevers beslutninger og udvikling af løsninger.

Din rolle

Du vil være med til at drive den grønne omstilling til gavn for hele samfundet. Her spiller virksomheder en vigtig rolle gennem elektrificering af deres bilflåder. For at kunne understøtte denne udvikling er det vigtigt, at vi driver omstillingen baseret på datadrevne beslutninger funderet i kundernes adfærd. Netop dét har vi brug for dig til at hjælpe med. 

Du vil være ansvarlig for at omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Hvad fyldes din dag med?

Rollen er hverken en ren Power BI-rolle eller en rolle, hvor du sidder og bygger tunge ML-modeller. Det er en rolle for dig, der vil være tæt på forretningen og være med til at løfte vores data modenheden i Erhverv, så vores beslutninger bliver skarpere, hurtigere og mindre afhængige af enkeltpersoner. I din hverdag vil du omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata, og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på, hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Konkret betyder det at du:


Driver overgangen fra manuelle Excel-processer til robuste, skalerbare og datadrevne beslutningsgrundlag – f.eks. inden for forecasting, segmentindsigter og prissætning.
Samarbejder med vores Data Enablement-team om at udvikle dataløsninger og omsætte forretningens behov til konkrete data- og analyseleverancer.
Udvikler og videreudvikler Power BI-appen “Clever Erhverv” samt andre rapporterings- og analyseværktøjer, så de bidrager til bedre beslutninger for forretningen.
Sparrer og rådgiver om data for vores produkt-, salgs- og supportteams.
Aarbejder sammen med vores kvalitative indsigtsrolle for at skabe et stærkt beslutningsgrundlag på tværs af kvantitative og kvalitative indsigter.



Dit team

Du bliver en del af teamet Forretningsudvikling for Erhverv. Vi er et udviklingsteam på ni personer med kompetencer inden for økonomiske og kvalitative indsigter, produktudvikling samt salgs- og erhvervsstrategi. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med den kvalitative indsigtsrolle, produktudviklerne og resten af erhvervsforretningen.

Du vil have berøringsflader med mange forskellige interessenter på tværs af erhvervsforretningen og navigere i et udviklende og tempofyldt område, hvor dine kompetencer vil være efterspurgte. Samtidig bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med Clevers øvrige specialister inden for kvalitative indsigter, hvor vi sparrer og vidensdeler.

Hvem er du?

Når vi forestiller os vores kommende kollega, ser vi dig, der har:


Fokus på beslutninger frem for rapporter og motiveres af at hjælpe forretningen med at forudse udviklingen og handle på den – ikke blot at visualisere historiske data.
Tekniske dybde for at indgå i en kvalificeret dialog med vores Data Enablement-team.
Kendskab til statistiske og datavidenskabelige metoder, kan arbejde i SQL og Databricks og kan vurdere kvaliteten af dataløsninger, selvom du ikke selv bygger dem.
Erfaring med Power BI og Excel (DAX er en fordel), og ser værktøjerne som middel til at skabe bedre beslutninger.
Stærke formidlingsevner til at oversætte mellem data og forretning og skabe struktur og klarhed for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
Lyst til at drive udvikling og være med til at løfte, hvordan vi arbejder datadrevet i Erhverv.



Vi er ekstra begejstrede, hvis du lytter aktivt, arbejder analytisk og kan skabe struktur og klarhed i komplekse dialoger.

Medledelseskompetencer


Du holder dit ego i skak og ved, at succes handler om at løfte i flok mod fælles mål  
Du tager naturligt ansvar for både dig selv og dine kollegaer, med omsorg og nærvær  
Du er en holdspiller, der ser arbejde som et samarbejde, ikke en konkurrence  



Har du sat mentale flueben hele vejen igennem?

Send gerne din ansøgning afsted allerede i dag – for vi kan godt finde på at lukke opslaget ned før tid, hvis vi møder det rigtige match. Vi håber, at vi gennem din ansøgning kan lære dig at kende og se, hvad du kan bidrage med. Hvis du bruger AI-værktøjer til inspiration, er det fint, men husk at personliggøre den for at skille dig ud.

Vi holder en lang velfortjent sommerferie væk fra computeren og er tilbage i uge 33, hvor du kan forvente at høre fra os. Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål i mellem tiden, så kontakt os på rekruttering@clever.dk .

Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på: www.clever.dk/velkommen eller vores Instagramkonto @liveticlever.

Er du nysgerrig på vores rekrutteringsproces kan du læse mere om den her: Rekrutteringsprocessen i Clever | Clever .

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

Title: Senior Data Scientist - Trifork Digital Health
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Hos Trifork designer, bygger og driver vi digitale løsninger til både den offentlige sundhedssektor og private medtech-virksomheder. Vores mål er at udvikle teknologi, der styrker både arbejdsgange og den enkelte patient og brugers møde med sundhedsvæsenet og sundhedsløsninger.

Vi søger nu 1-2 erfarne data scientists med stærkt forretningsfokus til vores Digital Health division i København. Du bliver en del af et fagligt stærkt team og får mulighed for at omsætte kunstig intelligens til praktiske, værdiskabende løsninger.

Om rollen

Som senior data scientist hos Trifork Digital Health kræver arbejdet lige dele teknisk ekspertise og forståelse for kundens forretning. Vores projekter spænder bredt – fra samfundskritisk sundheds-infrastruktur til innovativ og teknisk avanceret software inden for medtech. Fælles for dem er, at kvalitet, sikkerhed og driftsstabilitet er afgørende.

Vi arbejder med hele paletten af moderne AI – fra klassisk machine learning og prædiktive modeller til generativ og agentic AI – og anvender det både til at automatisere og løfte interne arbejdsgange og i kundevendte produkter og løsninger.

Vi arbejder tæt sammen med vores kunder – også fysisk. Du skal derfor trives med at være en synlig og nærværende del af kundens hverdag.

Du Kommer Til At


Tage end-to-end ansvar for data science- og AI-løsninger – fra behovsafklaring og løsningsdesign til implementering og drift
Anvende avancerede machine learning- og AI-teknikker til at løse komplekse forretningsproblemer for vores kunder
Designe og bygge robuste, skalerbare ML-løsninger og MLOps-pipelines, der kan sættes i produktion og vedligeholdes
Evaluere modeller og løsninger systematisk – det er mindst lige så vigtigt, at du kan forstå, hvordan og hvorfor et system fejler, og hvordan man undersøger det, som hvordan man bygger det
Arbejde med følsomme sundhedsdata under høje krav til sikkerhed, dokumentation og compliance – og sikre ansvarlig anvendelse af AI gennem solid AI governance
Samarbejde tæt med kunder, sundhedsfaglige eksperter og kolleger – og formidle resultater, muligheder og begrænsninger klart til både tekniske og ikke-tekniske modtagere
Bidrage til den strategiske retning for vores data science- og AI-initiativer, spotte nye muligheder og være med til at forme best practices
Dele din viden og være faglig sparringspartner for mindre erfarne kolleger


Vi søger dig, der har

Erfaring og baggrund


En kandidatgrad i data science, cognitive science, statistik, datalogi, matematik, fysik, ingeniørvidenskab eller lignende
Solid praktisk erfaring med data science og machine learning – gerne minimum 5 år
Erfaring fra sundhedssektoren, pharma eller medtech – eller stor interesse for området
Erfaring som konsulent eller med at arbejde tæt sammen med kunder
Erfaring med at sætte modeller i produktion – herunder MLOps og cloud-platforme
Erfaring med Docker/Kubernetes
Erfaring med LLM'er, generativ AI og gerne agentic AI
Forståelse for AI governance – herunder ansvarlig og etisk anvendelse af AI/ML-modeller i praksis


Personlige egenskaber


Er nysgerrig og løsningsorienteret
Trives med at arbejde tæt på kunden og tager gerne ansvar for både den faglige løsning og samarbejdet omkring den
Har blik for, at den bedste løsning ikke altid er den mest avancerede – men den, der skaber værdi for kunden
Kan formidle komplekse analyser, så de skaber et klart beslutningsgrundlag
Har styr på din AI-værktøjskasse og bruger den som en naturlig del af din egen arbejdsproces


Sprog


Flydende dansk og engelsk, mundtligt og skriftligt


Værktøjskassen


Python og det moderne ML-økosystem (fx scikit-learn, PyTorch)
LLM'er og frameworks til generativ og agentic AI
MLOps og cloud-platforme (Azure, AWS eller Google Cloud)
SQL og datamodellering
Docker


Hvad kan vi tilbyde?

Vores hverdag er præget af en åben og ærlig atmosfære. Vi lægger vægt på at skabe et udfordrende, men også sjovt, arbejdsmiljø. Vi arbejder i agile og tværfaglige teams med fokus på at hjælpe hinanden, og vi samarbejder tæt med vores kunder for at forstå deres behov og udfordringer. Trifork ligger i top 10 på Ingeniørens IT-Profilanalyse 2025 – en landsdækkende undersøgelse af Danmarks mest attraktive arbejdspladser for it-professionelle.

Vi tilbyder gode muligheder for faglig og personlig udvikling gennem projekter, interne tech-møder, Trifork Hackerdays, konferencer, sociale arrangementer og vores årlige teamtur.

Udover Dette Får Du


Et åbent og uformelt arbejdsmiljø
Mulighed for at tage initiativ
Frokostordning
Frisklavet italiensk kaffe
Kontor med nem adgang til offentlig transport
MacBook Pro eller high-end Dell laptop samt smartphone efter eget valg


Om Trifork

Trifork er en global teknologivirksomhed noteret på Nasdaq Copenhagen med over 1.100 medarbejdere i 16 lande. Vi designer, bygger og driver avancerede softwareløsninger inden for offentlig administration, sundhed, finansielle services, energi og luftfart.

Inden for Digital Health er Trifork en foretrukken partner, der med passion og innovation bygger digitale løsninger, som skaber værdi på tværs af sundhedssektoren. Vi udvider i 2026 vores tilstedeværelse til nye markeder i Canada og Tyskland og fortsætter med at styrke vores position som pioner inden for digital sundhed.

Interesseret?

Hvis du er interesseret i at søge stillingen, så send din ansøgning, CV og relevante dokumenter. Vi gennemgår ansøgninger og holder samtaler løbende, så søg gerne hurtigst muligt.

HTML:
Hos Trifork designer, bygger og driver vi digitale løsninger til både den offentlige sundhedssektor og private medtech-virksomheder. Vores mål er at udvikle teknologi, der styrker både arbejdsgange og den enkelte patient og brugers møde med sundhedsvæsenet og sundhedsløsninger.

Vi søger nu 1-2 erfarne data scientists med stærkt forretningsfokus til vores Digital Health division i København. Du bliver en del af et fagligt stærkt team og får mulighed for at omsætte kunstig intelligens til praktiske, værdiskabende løsninger.

Om rollen

Som senior data scientist hos Trifork Digital Health kræver arbejdet lige dele teknisk ekspertise og forståelse for kundens forretning. Vores projekter spænder bredt – fra samfundskritisk sundheds-infrastruktur til innovativ og teknisk avanceret software inden for medtech. Fælles for dem er, at kvalitet, sikkerhed og driftsstabilitet er afgørende.

Vi arbejder med hele paletten af moderne AI – fra klassisk machine learning og prædiktive modeller til generativ og agentic AI – og anvender det både til at automatisere og løfte interne arbejdsgange og i kundevendte produkter og løsninger.

Vi arbejder tæt sammen med vores kunder – også fysisk. Du skal derfor trives med at være en synlig og nærværende del af kundens hverdag.

Du Kommer Til At

  • Tage end-to-end ansvar for data science- og AI-løsninger – fra behovsafklaring og løsningsdesign til implementering og drift
  • Anvende avancerede machine learning- og AI-teknikker til at løse komplekse forretningsproblemer for vores kunder
  • Designe og bygge robuste, skalerbare ML-løsninger og MLOps-pipelines, der kan sættes i produktion og vedligeholdes
  • Evaluere modeller og løsninger systematisk – det er mindst lige så vigtigt, at du kan forstå, hvordan og hvorfor et system fejler, og hvordan man undersøger det, som hvordan man bygger det
  • Arbejde med følsomme sundhedsdata under høje krav til sikkerhed, dokumentation og compliance – og sikre ansvarlig anvendelse af AI gennem solid AI governance
  • Samarbejde tæt med kunder, sundhedsfaglige eksperter og kolleger – og formidle resultater, muligheder og begrænsninger klart til både tekniske og ikke-tekniske modtagere
  • Bidrage til den strategiske retning for vores data science- og AI-initiativer, spotte nye muligheder og være med til at forme best practices
  • Dele din viden og være faglig sparringspartner for mindre erfarne kolleger

Vi søger dig, der har

Erfaring og baggrund

  • En kandidatgrad i data science, cognitive science, statistik, datalogi, matematik, fysik, ingeniørvidenskab eller lignende
  • Solid praktisk erfaring med data science og machine learning – gerne minimum 5 år
  • Erfaring fra sundhedssektoren, pharma eller medtech – eller stor interesse for området
  • Erfaring som konsulent eller med at arbejde tæt sammen med kunder
  • Erfaring med at sætte modeller i produktion – herunder MLOps og cloud-platforme
  • Erfaring med Docker/Kubernetes
  • Erfaring med LLM'er, generativ AI og gerne agentic AI
  • Forståelse for AI governance – herunder ansvarlig og etisk anvendelse af AI/ML-modeller i praksis

Personlige egenskaber

  • Er nysgerrig og løsningsorienteret
  • Trives med at arbejde tæt på kunden og tager gerne ansvar for både den faglige løsning og samarbejdet omkring den
  • Har blik for, at den bedste løsning ikke altid er den mest avancerede – men den, der skaber værdi for kunden
  • Kan formidle komplekse analyser, så de skaber et klart beslutningsgrundlag
  • Har styr på din AI-værktøjskasse og bruger den som en naturlig del af din egen arbejdsproces

Sprog

  • Flydende dansk og engelsk, mundtligt og skriftligt

Værktøjskassen

  • Python og det moderne ML-økosystem (fx scikit-learn, PyTorch)
  • LLM'er og frameworks til generativ og agentic AI
  • MLOps og cloud-platforme (Azure, AWS eller Google Cloud)
  • SQL og datamodellering
  • Docker

Title: Lead Analytics Platform Developer
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us in this role where you’ll be the Business Application Responsible for the Analytics Workspace, a JupyterHub-based platform built on cloud Kubernetes and used across Trading & Revenue. You’ll co-develop the platform with Commercial Digital Technology, guide and test deliveries, and make sure the platform evolves in line with user needs across analytics, algo development, and model development.

Welcome to Analytics

You’ll be part of Data Science & Platforms, one of three teams in Trading & Revenue’s Analytics function, where you, together with your colleagues, will deliver the platforms and products behind trading and revenue decisions. You’ll work closely with quantitative developers, analysts, model developers, and Commercial Digital Technology to shape a reliable, scalable platform that supports both current users and new analytics capabilities. As a team, we value flexibility, experimentation, knowledge sharing, and collaboration. We care about building an inclusive environment where different perspectives help us create better solutions.

You’ll play an important role in:


owning the Analytics Workspace on the Trading & Revenue side, ensuring it remains reliable, relevant, and well aligned with business needs
co-developing platform capabilities with Commercial Digital Technology through coding, prototyping, reviewing pull requests, and defining acceptance tests
enabling strong platform foundations for analytics, algo development, and machine learning use cases, including compute, environments, data access, deployment, logging, and monitoring
supporting users through onboarding, day-to-day platform support, and close collaboration with stakeholders across Trading & Revenue
maintaining best practices, tools, templates, and ways of working for analytics engineering and the wider analytics community
driving the platform roadmap, including improvements, migrations, and adjacent capabilities such as machine learning, streaming and AI tooling.


To succeed in the role, you:


have strong Python development skills and apply sound engineering practices across testing, packaging, and CI/CD
bring hands-on experience with JupyterHub-based platforms, Kubernetes, Airflow and cloud infrastructure in Azure, AWS, or similar environments
work confidently across DevOps or MLOps topics and can contribute to platform design, delivery, and operational excellence
collaborate well with internal users and external engineering partners, and communicate clearly with both technical and business stakeholders
take ownership, build trust, and stay close to user needs while helping specialist teams contribute and co-own solutions
are motivated to grow your understanding of Trading & Revenue analytics, and you bring curiosity, structure, and a practical mindset to complex platform challenges.


Maybe you’ve read the above and can see you have some transferable skills, even though they don’t quite match all the points. If you think you can bring something to the team, we still encourage you to apply.

Shape the future with us

Send your application to us as soon as possible. We’ll be conducting interviews on a continuous basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we’ve found the right candidate.

As an applicant or employee, you may request reasonable work and position accommodation or adjustments via accommodation@orsted.com.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.

Position salary range:

For this position, the expected base pay range is kr58,000.00- kr87,000.00 for the advertised location. The offered base salary will be determined within this range based on relevant skills, experience, and expected role fulfilment within the stated pay range to ensure a fair and competitive offer. Where applicable, you may also be eligible for variable payment and a comprehensive benefits package.

Our benefits package:

For this position, employees who meet eligibility requirements have access to: employer‑paid pension (11% contribution), full insurance coverage (accident, life, critical illness, and health insurance); five weeks of paid holiday plus 10 additional paid days off per holiday year; a 3% holiday allowance; access to Ørsted Life & Fitness Vitality including fitness classes, physiotherapy, massage, and wellbeing programmes; a multimedia package (mobile phone, PC, internet); staff club membership with sport and cultural activities; and access to the company canteen (monthly lunch scheme or daily tickets).

Pre-employment background check:

Certain positions may require a background check as part of the hiring process. If applicable, you will be informed during the recruitment process. Background checks are conducted by an external provider in accordance with local legal requirements.

Title: Principal Data Scientist
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Location - Ballerup

Your new department

The Principal Data Scientist in the Commercial AI & Data department is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing advanced AI and machine learning algorithms to process and analyse structured and unstructured data from diverse sources. You will be part of a business area that drives impact across the full Novo Nordisk value chain. We strive to stay close to our stakeholders in the business to ensure we deliver on their needs, helping reach millions of people living with serious chronic diseases. Our focus is on being at the forefront within our three areas; ensuring the highest quality for patients, being a trusted tech leader and an environmentally responsible business.

Your Key Responsibilities Include


Define Data Science Strategy
Develop and influence the organization's data science vision and roadmap.
Collaborate with senior leaders and executives to align AI/ML initiatives with business priorities.
Help determine where data science can create the greatest strategic value.
Lead Innovation and Advanced Analytics
Design and champion innovative AI, machine learning, and advanced analytics solutions.
Solve highly complex business problems through advanced statistical and computational methods.
Evaluate and introduce emerging technologies and methodologies.
Serve as a Technical Authority
Act as the organization’s subject matter expert for data science.
Establish best practices, standards, and frameworks for model development and deployment.
Provide expert guidance on methodologies, tools, and architectural decisions.
Shape Business Decisions
Translate business opportunities and challenges into data-driven strategies.
Communicate insights, risks, and recommendations to executive leadership.
Influence investment decisions and enterprise-wide initiatives using evidence-based approaches.
Mentor and Develop Talent
Coach senior data scientists and technical leaders.
Build organizational capability through mentoring, knowledge sharing, and establishing communities of practice.
Help develop future leaders in data science.
Drive Cross-Functional Collaboration
Work across business functions, technology, engineering, analytics, product, compliance, and external partners.
Lead large-scale initiatives that span multiple teams and organizational boundaries.
Represent the IT Organization across the Stakeholders’ communities
Bring stakeholders together on the DS strategy and vision
Build external networks and stay at the forefront of AI and data science advancements.
Position the Enterprise AI org. as a leader in data-driven innovation.


Your New Department

The Data Scientist in the Commercial AI & Data department is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing advanced AI and machine learning algorithms to process and analyse structured and unstructured data from diverse sources. You will be entrusted with making independent decisions on the selection of suitable methodologies, and techniques for data analysis and model development.

Skills & Qualifications.


Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field. Ph.D. is preferred.
A proven track record of innovation and thought leadership.
Minimum 10 years of experience in data science, machine learning, or AI, with a focus on developing and applying advanced algorithms.
Deep expertise in cutting-edge data science methodologies and technologies
Strong research capabilities and the ability to translate research into business value
Experience developing enterprise-scale data strategies and AI roadmaps.
Ability to provide visionary leadership for the data science function
Broad understanding of pharma industry and Commercial business processes
Strong awareness of ethical and responsible AI practices
Experience influencing strategic decisions through data science and analytics.
In-depth knowledge of machine learning concepts, data modeling techniques and AI solution design.
Experience working with cross-functional teams (data engineering, ML engineering, analytics, product, operations).
Familiarity with MLOps tools and practices (e.g., MLflow, model registries, CI/CD for models) and cloud‑agnostic deployment patterns.
In‑depth understanding and practical experience with architecture, implementation, DevOps and support for AI solutions.
Experience working with cross‑functional teams (data engineering, ML engineering, analytics, product, operations) and external vendors.
Experience in pharmaceutical or regulated industries is beneficial but not mandatory.


Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

There is, of course, more on offer here than the uniqueness of our culture and the extraordinary results we produce. Being part of a global healthcare company means opportunities to learn and develop are all around us, while our benefits are designed with your career and life stage in mind.

Salary

For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 847,000.00 to 1,245,100.00 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.

The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Title: AI Engineer
Place: Hedehusene, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Req Number: 120122

Time Type: Full Time

At DSV, we keep global supply chains moving – and increasingly, this includes how we use AI to support critical operations, decisions, and customer experiences across the world.

We are looking for an AI Engineer who is motivated to turn emerging AI capabilities into reliable, high-impact solutions. You are curious, solution-oriented, and driven to push boundaries, drive continuous improvement, and build production-grade language systems powered by large language models.

Your Responsibilities And Impact

You join our AI Language Systems team that develops high-impact AI products and capabilities used across the organization. We work in agile squads aligned with different products and business domains, collaborating closely with product, research, and operational teams.

We support critical business operations by experimenting with, evaluating, and applying advanced AI, LLM, and NLP methods to solve real problems and move the business forward. Our work goes beyond research prototypes and notebook-based experimentation. We design, build, deploy, and operate reliable, production-grade AI solutions that perform at scale and deliver measurable business value.

As an AI Engineer, you help shape how advanced AI and large language models are applied in real business contexts. The role combines applied AI research and software engineering. You will independently deliver AI and GenAI features of moderate complexity, owning scoped work from requirements clarification through experimentation, implementation, deployment, and initial production support.

You will work on RAG pipelines, agentic search, prompt optimization, model evaluation, fine-tuning workflows, and emerging integration patterns such as Model Context Protocol and agent-to-agent communication.

Key Responsibilities



Independently deliver scoped AI, GenAI, NLP, and RAG features of moderate complexity
Develop and enhance RAG pipelines, including document parsing and ingestion, chunking and metadata strategies, query transformation, retrieval and ranking, response generation and grounding
Build GenAI features using LLM APIs, structured prompting, and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LangGraph, DSPy, etc.)
Evaluate AI system performance using practical methods such as retrieval metrics, response quality assessment, hallucination analysis, latency measurement, cost analysis, and failure-case testing
Understand engineering trade-offs across model quality, latency, cost, reliability, maintainability, and implementation complexity
Own features end-to-end – from clarification and experimentation to deployment and initial support
Translate requirements into user stories and provide implementation plans, as well as own features end-to-end throughout the software development lifecycle - from clarification and experimentation to deployment and initial support
Identify risks, dependencies, and data limitations early and propose workable solutions
Challenge unclear requirements and contribute with pragmatic, value-driven alternatives
Stay close to new developments in LLMs, RAG, prompt optimization, model evaluation, fine-tuning, and applied AI frameworks, and assess where they create real business impact



What You Bring

Ideally, you have strong hands-on AI engineering experience, understand the fundamentals of modern AI language systems, and can translate product and technical requirements into reliable production implementations with limited guidance.

To succeed and thrive in the role, you bring:



A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, AI, Machine Learning, or similar- or equivalent professional experience
3+ years of professional AI engineering/applied data science experience, including hands-on experience with AI, NLP, machine learning, deep learning, or language-model-based applications
Strong Python skills and experience building clean, maintainable, and production-ready software
Hands-on experience with GenAI or LLM-based solutions or open-source models
Solid understanding of software engineering practices (testing, CI/CD, version control, etc.)
Experience with model evaluation, monitoring, or experiment tracking tools (i.e., MLflow or similar)
Ability to work in cross-functional, agile teams and communicate clearly in English



Nice to have:



Experience with cloud platforms such as Google Cloud or similar
Familiarity with RAG architectures, embeddings, vector databases, and retrieval techniques
Exposure to fine-tuning or model optimization approaches
Experience with Kedro for building modular, reproducible data and ML pipelines, and KServe for scalable, production-grade model deployment and inference on Kubernetes
Knowledge of agentic workflows, tool-calling systems, agentic search, MCP, or A2A integration patterns



What This Role Offers



Ownership of real AI solutions used at scale in a global organisation
The opportunity to turn cutting-edge technology into tangible business impact
Collaboration with international teams across products, engineering, and operations
A dynamic environment focused on learning, improvement, and shared success
Opportunities to grow your skills and shape how AI is applied across DSV



Why DSV

You will join a global organisation with more than 150,000 colleagues across 90+ countries, working together to keep supply chains moving worldwide.

At DSV, movement creates opportunity – and your work helps transform how technology supports that movement every day. We foster a culture of curiosity, ownership, continuous improvement, and shared success, with a strong focus on the growth of both our products and our people.

Interested?

Apply today and be part of a team that keeps the world moving.

We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply early. Please note that while the application deadline is 25 July 2026, we may close the position sooner if we find the right candidate. No cover letter is needed; just include a short motivation in your CV.

If you would like to know more about the role, you are welcome to contact Manager, Enterprise Business Development, Vasileios Iosifidis at vasileios.iosifidis@dsv.com

DSV – Global transport and logistics

Working at DSV means playing in a different league.

As a global leader in transport and logistics, we have been on an extraordinary journey of growth. Let’s grow together as we continue to innovate, digitalise and build on our achievements.

With close to 150,000 colleagues in over 90 countries, we work every day to offer solid services and meet our customers’ needs and help them achieve their goals. We know that the best way to achieve this is by bringing in new talent, fresh perspectives and ambitious individuals like you.

At DSV, performance is in our DNA. We don’t just work – we aim to shape the future of logistics. This ambition fuels a dynamic environment built on collaboration with world-class team players, accountability and action. We value inclusivity, embrace different cultures and respect the dignity and rights of every individual. If you want to make an impact, be trusted by customers and grow your career in a forward-thinking company – this is the place to be.

Start here. Go anywhere

Visit dsv.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook.

Your Responsibilities And Impact

You join our AI Language Systems team that develops high-impact AI products and capabilities used across the organization. We work in agile squads aligned with different products and business domains, collaborating closely with product, research, and operational teams.

We support critical business operations by experimenting with, evaluating, and applying advanced AI, LLM, and NLP methods to solve real problems and move the business forward. Our work goes beyond research prototypes and notebook-based experimentation. We design, build, deploy, and operate reliable, production-grade AI solutions that perform at scale and deliver measurable business value.

As an AI Engineer, you help shape how advanced AI and large language models are applied in real business contexts. The role combines applied AI research and software engineering. You will independently deliver AI and GenAI features of moderate complexity, owning scoped work from requirements clarification through experimentation, implementation, deployment, and initial production support.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Sønderborg, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Would you like to work for a company with solutions that contributes to Saab’s mission of keeping people and society safe?

Would you like to be a part of shaping the Future of Digital Engineering and building the Future of Engineering?

Engineering is evolving rapidly. Model Based Systems Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, connected engineering data, and digital collaboration are transforming how complex systems are designed and delivered.

We are looking for a Data Engineer to help build the data foundation that enables modern engineering. You will connect engineering data, improve digital workflows, and prepare the organization for AI enabled engineering.

Your role

You will:


Develop data pipelines and system integrations across engineering tools
Improve data quality, consistency, governance, and traceability
Support Digital Thread and MBSE initiatives
Prepare engineering data for AI and advanced analytics
Design scalable data models and dashboards
Identify opportunities to automate engineering processes
Collaborate with engineers, IT, and business stakeholders to deliver practical solutions



Your Profile

You have experience in one or more of the following areas:


Data Engineering
Software Engineering
Systems Engineering
Data Science
Computer Science
Information Technology



Experience with Python, SQL, APIs, cloud platforms, Power BI, graph databases, engineering lifecycle tools, or AI technologies is an advantage but not a requirement.

More important than your exact background is your ability to solve complex problems, connect information, and turn data into business value.

We Are Looking For Someone Who


Thinks analytically and works in a structured way
Enjoys collaborating across technical disciplines
Is curious about new technologies and continuous improvement
Can translate engineering challenges into digital solutions
Takes ownership and delivers pragmatic results



Why Join Us

This role offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of engineering, data, and emerging technologies.

Your work will help accelerate Digital Engineering, strengthen MBSE adoption, improve engineering efficiency, and establish the foundation for future AI capabilities.

If you want to build solutions that will influence how engineering is performed in the years ahead, we would like to hear from you.

What can you expect from us?

Saab Danmark is a company that is growing and you will therefore experience a company that is dynamic and able to adapt. In the Saab group we have a saying” Business is done between people”, and this is also the environment you can expect here. We offer:


Initial learning process with mentorship and support
Good career and development opportunities in a professional company
A vibrant and international atmosphere with colleagues from all over the world
Benefits that support our employees’ wellbeing
A variety of social activities, lunch scheme, pension as well as flexible work conditions
Focus on our teams, good cooperation and giving our employees the ability to take ownership of their tasks.



Candidates must be able to obtain a security clearance by the Danish Defence Intelligence Service. This entails that candidates, as a minimum, have had residency within NATO/EU for the last 7 years.

About Saab Danmark A/S

Saab in Denmark is part of the Saab Group with headquarters in Stockholm, Sweden. At the same time, Saab Danmark is an independent legal company with a flat organisational structure. Our main business area is delivering integrated communication solutions to military markets in Denmark and abroad.

You can find further information about our integrated communication solution TactiCall, at www.saab.com/naval.

Title: AI Udvikler
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Bliv AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT og vær med til at udvikle fremtidens AI-løsninger til kommunerne!




Har du lyst til at udvikle ansvarlige, sikre og værdiskabende AI-løsninger – og arbejde med de nyeste værktøjer i et stærkt fagligt miljø? Så har vi netop den rette mulighed for dig.




Et stærkt it-fællesskab

I KOMBIT har de danske kommuner et fælles omdrejningspunkt med stærke og specialiserede kompetencer på digitaliseringsområdet. Vi er et kommunalt ejet it-fællesskab, der er ansvarlig for at indkøbe, forvalte, drifte og videreudvikle tidssvarende og stabile it-løsninger og -infrastruktur til gavn for borgere og virksomheder. Sammen med kommunerne og it-leverandørerne arbejder vi for, at borgere og virksomheder i Danmark får en bedre service og billigere og mere sammenhængende it-løsninger. KOMBITs ambition er at sikre fortsat kommunal digital bevægelse - i dag og i fremtiden.




Vi har et stærkt fokus på at levere ansvarlige og sikre AI-løsninger, der kan komme i produktion og skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Dine primære ansvarsområder

Du bliver tilknyttet specialet Design & Arkitektur og en del af KOMBITs fokuserede AI core team. Her arbejder vi sammen med bl.a. KL, kommunerne, leverandører samt interne og eksterne eksperter om at udvikle, modne og udbrede AI-løsninger i den kommunale sektor.

Vi søger udviklere, der vil være med til at bygge konkrete AI-løsninger fra idé til produktion. Det kan være, at du har en baggrund inden for data science, erfaring som full stack-udvikler eller stærke kompetencer inden for DevOps, CI/CD og moderne udviklingsmiljøer. Det vigtigste er, at du har lyst til at arbejde praktisk og nysgerrigt med AI-teknologi, softwareudvikling og løsninger, der kan skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Som AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT kommer du bl.a. til at arbejde med udvikling, test, integration, drift og videreudvikling af AI-løsninger. Vi anvender AI aktivt i alle faser af udviklingen – fra analyse, design og kodning til test, dokumentation, kvalitetssikring og drift. Du vil derfor også få mulighed for at arbejde med AI-agenter og andre nye værktøjer, der kan styrke både udviklingsprocessen og de løsninger, vi leverer.




Du kan eksempelvis blive involveret i arbejdet med vores AI Assistent, som hjælper kommunale sagsbehandlere med at anvende fagsystemer og søge information i lovgivningen, og som også anvendes internt i KOMBIT. Derudover kan du bidrage til udvikling af nye AI-produkter, fælles komponenter og platforme, screening af nye use cases, dataanalyse, evaluering, overvågning og sikker drift af AI-løsninger.




Vi er på udkig efter flere nye kollegaer til teamet, så uanset om du primært ser dig selv som data scientist, full stack-udvikler, DevOps-profil eller AI-nysgerrig softwareudvikler, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.




Fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv

Vi tilbyder dig et job, hvor omdrejningspunktet er samarbejde på tværs, og hvor du vil arbejde med nogle af Danmarks største it-løsninger. Samtidig bliver du en del af en ambitiøs virksomhed, der står i udviklingens tegn, både hvad angår de mange eksisterende løsninger samt forventningen om en fremtid med endnu bredere portefølje inden for digitalisering og it-dreven innovation i kommunerne.




I KOMBIT er det vigtigt, at alle medarbejdere finder en god balance mellem arbejde og privatliv. Vi skaber motiverende rammer, der giver medarbejderne frihed til at planlægge og løse deres opgaver ud fra individuelle behov. Af samme grund vil du hurtigt opleve, at der er højt til loftet og rig mulighed for at sætte dit præg i en hver fyldt med spændende opgaver. Vi har som organisation fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv i balance – vi kalder det LiFE!

Lyder det som noget for dig?




Så tag første skridt mod en spændende karriere i frontlinjen for digital udvikling, og send os dit CV senest d. 2. august 2026. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at sende en mail til Chef for Design & Arkitektur, Lars Vraa (LVR@kombit.dk).




Vi screener relevante kandidater, hvor vi spørger ind til motivationen for at søge stillingen samt forventninger til vilkår for at sikre, at vi finder den bedst egnede kandidat til stillingen.




Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Title: AI Capability Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Copenhagen, Denmark

About The Job

Are you interested in helping shape and strengthen AI capabilities within a global financial organisation?

As an AI Capability Engineer, you will work with Saxo’s Engagement Brain — an AI driven engine for personalised, scalable client engagement. You will turn data and AI into tangible tools that our Relationship Managers and Client Service teams will rely on. In this role, you will make an impact and contribute to one of Saxo’s most significant client-facing transformations.

This position is located at Saxo’s Headquarters in Hellerup and is within the Client Experience team. This role reports directly to the Executive Director of Client Experience.

As an AI Capability Engineer, you will collaborate with Relationship Managers, Client Service Leads, and Journey Owners, to translate user feedback into product improvements. Saxo's central Data Platform team will support you on performance, governance, and shared infrastructure. You will own your capabilities and how they land in the business.

The ambition is for you to progressively deliver the wider engagement brain: from Relationship Manager tools today, Client Service tools next, to AI-driven engagement reaching clients across every Saxo channel. You will not just build the next capability; you will also help shape the pattern that Saxo’s client-facing AI grows from.

Responsibilities


Own the rollout and implementation of the first AI sales-tech capability from pilot to a hardened, centrally run product for all Saxo Relationship Managers
Build upon the same foundation to deliver additional AI capabilities for Relationship Managers, Client Service, Journey Owners, and eventually clients directly
Design and maintain the data foundation, owning the curated data model and semantic layer that AI capabilities read from
Operate AI capabilities in production with a focus on cost, quality, and dependability across all users and clients
Convert user feedback into lasting system improvements, embedding expert judgement into repeatable system rules
Ensure all output remains defensible by working with Commercial Finance, Risk, Compliance, and Legal, staying on the right side of Saxo’s execution-only advice line
Stay closely connected with users to maintain fast, high-quality feedback loops as the team and scope grow


Your profile

You have solid experience working with AI and data and understand how they can support business success. You have strong communication skills and can clearly explain technical information to commercial colleagues. You care about grounded, dependable AI products over flashy demos.


7-10 years of experience, with a minimum of 3 years’ experience within AI/ML applied to production business systems
Experience building and running data pipelines, automation, and AI capabilities to production standard
Experience with Python and modern data platforms (dbt, Snowflake, Databricks or equivalent)
Business and data fluency in trading or comparable financial services
Financial data literacy — portfolios, positions, transactions, KYC, market data
Prompt engineering at production scale — versioning, evaluation, output filtering (not experimentation)
Data privacy engineering with AI-specific considerations (GDPR)
Observability for AI systems — drift detection, cost monitoring, evaluation pipelines


Soft Skills


Direct communication – You can explain what you built, what it does, and what its limits are to various stakeholders without overselling the solution.
Comfortable working with ambiguity
Curious about the business
Judgment on when to ship vs. iterate
Ability to work with a variety of stakeholders, including CxO, Risk, and Compliance leaders, and to communicate challenges in their language
Willingness to challenge stakeholders when needed


It Will Be An Advantage If You Have


Prior experience with LLM applications in a regulated environment
Experience with agent frameworks (MCP, tool-use, retrieval-augmented generation)


Please note that due to the summer holiday period, responses will not be sent during July. Interviews are expected to be held in August.

We get curious people invested in the world

When you work at Saxo, you become a Saxonian and part of a purpose-driven organisation, where good ideas are always taken seriously, and where you can make a true impact. We are invested in your development, and you can expect a robust career from day one when you join Saxo – no matter which role you take on.

You will join 2,500 other ambitious colleagues across 11 countries and become part of an international organisation. Working in Saxo, you will get to meet colleagues from many different cultures and backgrounds, and you should know that we value diversity and inclusion and see it as a genuine source of strength to drive growth, foster innovation and position us for long-term success.

We encourage an open feedback culture and supportive team environments enabling employees to grow and fulfil their career aspirations.

HTML:
Copenhagen, Denmark

About The Job

Are you interested in helping shape and strengthen AI capabilities within a global financial organisation?

As an AI Capability Engineer, you will work with Saxo’s Engagement Brain — an AI driven engine for personalised, scalable client engagement. You will turn data and AI into tangible tools that our Relationship Managers and Client Service teams will rely on. In this role, you will make an impact and contribute to one of Saxo’s most significant client-facing transformations.

This position is located at Saxo’s Headquarters in Hellerup and is within the Client Experience team. This role reports directly to the Executive Director of Client Experience.

As an AI Capability Engineer, you will collaborate with Relationship Managers, Client Service Leads, and Journey Owners, to translate user feedback into product improvements. Saxo's central Data Platform team will support you on performance, governance, and shared infrastructure. You will own your capabilities and how they land in the business.

The ambition is for you to progressively deliver the wider engagement brain: from Relationship Manager tools today, Client Service tools next, to AI-driven engagement reaching clients across every Saxo channel. You will not just build the next capability; you will also help shape the pattern that Saxo’s client-facing AI grows from.

Responsibilities

  • Own the rollout and implementation of the first AI sales-tech capability from pilot to a hardened, centrally run product for all Saxo Relationship Managers
  • Build upon the same foundation to deliver additional AI capabilities for Relationship Managers, Client Service, Journey Owners, and eventually clients directly
  • Design and maintain the data foundation, owning the curated data model and semantic layer that AI capabilities read from
  • Operate AI capabilities in production with a focus on cost, quality, and dependability across all users and clients
  • Convert user feedback into lasting system improvements, embedding expert judgement into repeatable system rules
  • Ensure all output remains defensible by working with Commercial Finance, Risk, Compliance, and Legal, staying on the right side of Saxo’s execution-only advice line
  • Stay closely connected with users to maintain fast, high-quality feedback loops as the team and scope grow

Your profile

You have solid experience working with AI and data and understand how they can support business success. You have strong communication skills and can clearly explain technical information to commercial colleagues. You care about grounded, dependable AI products over flashy demos.

  • 7-10 years of experience, with a minimum of 3 years’ experience within AI/ML applied to production business systems
  • Experience building and running data pipelines, automation, and AI capabilities to production standard
  • Experience with Python and modern data platforms (dbt, Snowflake, Databricks or equivalent)
  • Business and data fluency in trading or comparable financial services
  • Financial data literacy — portfolios, positions, transactions, KYC, market data
  • Prompt engineering at production scale — versioning, evaluation, output filtering (not experimentation)
  • Data privacy engineering with AI-specific considerations (GDPR)
  • Observability for AI systems — drift detection, cost monitoring, evaluation pipelines

Soft Skills

  • Direct communication – You can explain what you built, what it does, and what its limits are to various stakeholders without overselling the solution.
  • Comfortable working with ambiguity
  • Curious about the business
  • Judgment on when to ship vs. iterate
  • Ability to work with a variety of stakeholders, including CxO, Risk, and Compliance leaders, and to communicate challenges in their language
  • Willingness to challenge stakeholders when needed

It Will Be An Advantage If You Have

  • Prior experience with LLM applications in a regulated environment
  • Experience with agent frameworks (MCP, tool-use, retrieval-augmented generation)

Please note that due to the summer holiday period, responses will not be sent during July. Interviews are expected to be held in August.

We get curious people invested in the world

When you work at Saxo, you become a Saxonian and part of a purpose-driven organisation, where good ideas are always taken seriously, and where you can make a true impact. We are invested in your development, and you can expect a robust career from day one when you join Saxo – no matter which role you take on.

You will join 2,500 other ambitious colleagues across 11 countries and become part of an international organisation. Working in Saxo, you will get to meet colleagues from many different cultures and backgrounds, and you should know that we value diversity and inclusion and see it as a genuine source of strength to drive growth, foster innovation and position us for long-term success.

We encourage an open feedback culture and supportive team environments enabling employees to grow and fulfil their career aspirations.

When you bring passion, curiosity, drive and team spirit, your learning journey will be dynamic and your career opportunities in Saxo will be immense.

At Saxo we don’t just offer a job – we offer an opportunity to invest in your future!

Title: Scientific Software Developer — Data Reduction Team
Place: Kongens Lyngby, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Scientific Software Developer — Data Reduction Team




The European Spallation Source (ESS) is building the world’s most powerful neutron source, enabling research in materials science, energy, health, and the environment. ESS is expanding the Data Reduction Team at the Data Management and Scientific Computing Centre (DMSC), located just north of Copenhagen in Denmark. We are seeking outstanding candidates for one scientific software developer position.




About the team




We are six developers responsible for the software that turns raw detector data into scientifically usable datasets. This process, known as data reduction, produces structured datasets for downstream analysis software. We develop Scipp (scipp.github.io) and related libraries, working primarily in Python. With ten new ESS instruments coming online next year, our software will play an important role in supporting their scientific commissioning and operations. As a small team, we work on problems that are both technically challenging and central to the success of the facility.




About the role




The work spans library development, scientific workflow design, performance engineering, and user interfaces. You will work closely with neutron scientists, distil requirements from them, determine what belongs in the core library versus a downstream package, and frequently decide what not to build. There is room to specialize over time, but in your first year you should expect to touch various parts of the codebase.




Our team uses AI coding tools heavily but tries to be smart about it. To us, using AI is about amplifying human thought, not outsourcing it. We see these tools as aids for exploration, iteration, and productivity — not replacements for engineering judgement.




About you




We care more about how you think than which tools you have used. We do not have a fixed technology checklist. What matters:




You have shipped software that real users depend on.
You think critically about code quality and welcome constructive discussion and alternative ideas
You can engage with scientific problems on their own terms — a physics background is welcome but not required.
You can take ownership of an area, work with scientists to gather requirements, and make decisions with incomplete information.
You are comfortable working in English in an international team.




Experience with the scientific Python ecosystem, performance engineering, or work at neutron/photon sources is beneficial but not required. Familiarity with technologies and practices such as Jupyter, C++, CI/CD, integration testing, or systems architecture would also be valuable.




What we offer

DMSC is located on the DTU campus in Kongens Lyngby, north of Copenhagen, while the ESS neutron facility is based in Lund, Sweden. Together, they form a unique cross-border scientific environment in the Øresund region.




You will join a dynamic and highly collaborative international workplace with colleagues representing nearly 60 nationalities across ESS and DMSC. We offer opportunities for professional development, and close interaction between computational science, instrumentation, and large-scale experiments.




Benefits include 25 + 5 annual leave days in addition to public and company holidays, an attractive pension scheme, preventative healthcare benefits, and relocation support for international hires where applicable.




This is a full-time, permanent position based in Kongens Lyngby, Denmark, and we need someone who can start as soon as practically possible.




Learn more about living and working in Scandinavia: https://ess.eu/living-working-scandinavia.

For further information




For information about the position, contact the Group Leader for Research Software Engineering (RSE), Fredrik Bolmsten, at fredrik.bolmsten@ess.eu




For recruitment-related questions, contact Recruitment Officer Janne Erwolter at janne.erwolter@ess.eu.




How to apply

Please submit your application, with cover letter and CV, no later than 26 August 2026.

As part of your cover letter, please pick one of these two questions and answer it in your application:




Tell us about a technical decision you made that you would make differently today, and what changed your mind.
Tell us about a piece of software you admire, not because of what it does, but because of how it is built, and what you learned from looking at it closely.




We’re genuinely interested in your response; it helps us understand your technical judgement and curiosity.




We look forward to receiving your application.
